Author: Ivan DelSol (@Ivan on forum)

You must have this in your project for TDAbleton Components to work. This package contains all Components of the TDAbleton system, or you can drag them from the TDAbleton Components folder in the palette. 

For help see docs.derivative.ca/TDAbleton

text.content:
2
*�����������������$f# AbletonMIDIExt

import socket
from copy import deepcopy
TDF = op.TDModules.mod.TDFunctions
TDAbletonCompBaseExt = \
					mod('abletonBase/TDAbletonCompBaseExt').TDAbletonCompBaseExt

class AbletonMIDIExt(TDAbletonCompBaseExt):
	"""
	AbletonMIDIExt works with TDA_MIDI Max Device to get MIDI notes
	"""
	def __init__(self, ownerComp):
		# The component to which this extension is attached
		self.OSCDeviceInCHOP = ownerComp.op('oscin_device')
		self.NoteDataCHOP = ownerComp.op('noteData')
		self.DefaultNotesCHOP = ownerComp.op('defaultNotes')
		self.NoteSplitterCHOP = ownerComp.op('noteSplitter')
		self.NoteSplitterCHOP.clear()
		self.LastNoteSplitterCHOP = ownerComp.op('lastNoteSplitter')
		self.LastNoteSplitterCHOP.clear()
		TDF.createProperty(self, 'deviceOutAddress', '', dependable=True)
		TDAbletonCompBaseExt.__init__(self, ownerComp)
		self.ownerComp.par.Defaultnoterange1.enable = \
							self.ownerComp.par.Showdefaultnotechannels.eval()
		run('op(' + str(self.ownerComp.id) + \
									').ext.AbletonMIDIExt.checkDeviceEnable()'\
					' if op(' + str(self.ownerComp.id) + ') else None',
					delayFrames=1, delayRef=op.TDResources)

	def SendMIDI(self, *args):
		"""
		Send a MIDI command to the connected TDA MIDI device.
		Arguments are a MIDI command string and 0-2 int data items:
			'note' <pitch value> <velocity>
			'pressure' <aftertouch pressure> <pitch value>
			'control' <control value> <controller number>
			'program' <program change value>
			'aftertouch' <aftertouch pressure>
			'pitchbend' <pitchbend amount -1.0 to 1.0>
			'channel' <MIDI channel>
			'flush' (no arguments, stops all MIDI notes)
		"""
		try:
			self.TDAbletonComp.SendMaxOSC(self.deviceOutAddress, args)
		except:
			if self.deviceOutAddress == '':
				raise Exception(self.ownerComp.path +
											": TDA MIDI device not connected.")
			raise

	def updateLOMPar(self, lomPar, lomInfo):
		"""
		Apply filters to base method.
		Track will be filtered for midi tracks.
		Device will be filtered for TDA_MIDI M4L devices only

		Base method:
		Updates a Live Object Model parameter (Track, Device, Parameter)
		lomPar is the parameter
		lomInfo is the associated dictionary from self.SongInfo

		 menuLabel is LO.name, menuName is LO pointer or
		 	'None' if None
		 	'Not_Found' if  not found
		 if lomInfo is None, set val to 'None' with no other menu options
		"""
		newLOMInfo = dict()
		# only include tracks with midi inputs
		if lomPar.name == 'Track' and lomInfo:
			for chainInfo in lomInfo.values():
				if chainInfo['hasMIDIInput']:
					newLOMInfo[chainInfo['name']] = chainInfo
		# only include TDA_MIDI devices
		elif lomInfo and (lomPar.name == 'Device'
						or lomPar.name.endswith('device')):
			pickDevice = None
			for deviceInfo in lomInfo.values():
				if 'ID_TDA_MIDI' in deviceInfo['aPars'] \
						or deviceInfo['chainType']:
					newLOMInfo[deviceInfo['name']] = deviceInfo
					if pickDevice is None and not deviceInfo['chainType']:
						pickDevice = deviceInfo
			# use first available device if current value not found
			if pickDevice:
				label = self.getMenuLabel(lomPar)
				if label.endswith(' (Not Found)'):
					label = label[:-12]
				# if newLOMInfo and (label not in newLOMInfo):
				# 	# hack the valid device name into menu
				# 	labels = lomPar.menuLabels
				# 	labels[lomPar.menuIndex] = pickDevice['name']
				# 	lomPar.menuLabels = labels
		# include all chains
		elif lomPar.name.startswith('Chain'):
			newLOMInfo = lomInfo
		return TDAbletonCompBaseExt.updateLOMPar(self, lomPar, newLOMInfo)

	def setupListeners(self):
		TDAbletonCompBaseExt.setupListeners(self)

		chainInfo = self.LOMParInfo(self.FinalChainPar)
		self.ownerComp.par.Adddevice.enable = chainInfo \
											and self.FinalChainPar == 'Track'

		# make sure we have a valid ableton parameter
		deviceInfo = self.maxDeviceInfo
		if not deviceInfo:
			self.ownerComp.par.Deviceon = False
			self.ownerComp.par.Deviceon.enable = False
			self.ownerComp.par.Devicename = ''
			self.ownerComp.par.Devicename.enable = False
			self.OSCDeviceInCHOP.par.oscaddressscope = ''
			self.deviceOutAddress = ''
			self.checkDeviceEnable()
			return

		maxTrackID = self.MaxTrackID()
		lomExpression = deviceInfo['lomExpression']
		returnAddressBase = deviceInfo['returnAddress']

		# midi channel name
		chanName = '/midi/' + maxTrackID + '/' + deviceInfo['lomInfo']['name']
		midiScope = chanName + '/*'
		self.OSCDeviceInCHOP.par.oscaddressscope = midiScope

		self.deviceOutAddress = chanName

		# make sure midi send device has correct address.
		self.sendOSC('/tda/command', ['setDeviceNetworking', lomExpression])

		# other info from MIDI device
		ownerId = self.ownerComp.id
		# DeviceOn
		self.ownerComp.par.Deviceon.enable = True
		deviceOnAddress = returnAddressBase + '/Device_On/value'
		listener = (lomExpression + '.parameters[0]', 'value',
													deviceOnAddress, ownerId)
		self.addListener(listener, self.ownerComp.par.Deviceon)
		self.checkDeviceEnable()
		# name
		self.ownerComp.par.Devicename.enable = True
		self.ownerComp.par.Devicename = deviceInfo['lomInfo']['name']
		nameAddress = '/info/listener' + returnAddressBase + '/name'
		listener = (lomExpression, 'name', nameAddress, ownerId)
		self.addListener(listener)

	def onAbletonNotify(self, info):
		TDAbletonCompBaseExt.onAbletonNotify(self, info)
		# Set midi ports when connected
		if info['notificationType'] == 'connected':
			self.checkDeviceEnable()
			self.OSCDeviceInCHOP.par.port = self.TDAbletonComp.par.Inport.eval()
		elif info['notificationType'] == 'disconnected':
			self.checkDeviceEnable()
		elif info['notificationType'] == 'lomNameChanged':
			changedObj = info
			if str(changedObj['ptr']) == self.ownerComp.par.Device.eval():
				self.ownerComp.par.Devicename = changedObj['name']
				validName = self.validChannelName(changedObj['name'])
				if info['name'] != validName:
					self.setDeviceName(validName)

	def clearOSCIn(self):
		self.NoteSplitterCHOP.clear()
		self.OSCDeviceInCHOP.par.resetpulse.pulse()
		TDAbletonCompBaseExt.clearOSCIn(self)
		self.NoteDataCHOP.par.value0 = -1
		self.NoteDataCHOP.par.value1 = -1

	def setDefaultNoteRange(self):
		self.setDefaultRange('Defaultnoterange', 'note_')

	def setDefaultCCRange(self):
		self.setDefaultRange('Defaultccrange', 'control_')

	def setDefaultRange(self, parName, suffix):
		minPar = self.ownerComp.par[parName + '1']
		maxPar = self.ownerComp.par[parName + '2']
		channels = self.OSCDeviceInCHOP.chans()
		min = 100000
		max = -1
		for c in channels:			
			if (id := c.name.split('/')[-1]).startswith(suffix):
				num = int(id.split('_')[1])
				if num < min:
					min = num
				if num > max:
					max = num
		if max != -1:
			minPar.val = min
			maxPar.val = max

	def onParPulse(self, par):
		if par.name == 'Adddevice':
			if not self.TDAbletonComp.Connected:
				return
			TDAbletonCompBaseExt.addDevice(self, 'TDA_MIDI.amxd', 'TdaMIDI')
		elif par.name == 'Settorecentlyplayed':
			self.setDefaultNoteRange()
		elif par.name == 'Settorecentlyused':
			self.setDefaultCCRange()
		else:
			TDAbletonCompBaseExt.onParPulse(self, par)

	def onParValueChange(self, par, val, prev):
		if par.name == 'Devicename':
			# make sure device name is a valid channel name or there'll be
			# problems receiving data
			deviceInfo = self.maxDeviceInfo
			if deviceInfo:
				val = self.validChannelName(val)
				if par.eval() != val or deviceInfo['lomInfo']['name'] != val:
					self.setDeviceName(val)
		elif par.name == 'Connect':
			self.checkDeviceEnable()
			TDAbletonCompBaseExt.onParValueChange(self, par, val, prev)
		elif par.name == 'Stripdeviceprefixsegments':
			self.OSCDeviceInCHOP.par.resetpulse.pulse()
			if self.ownerComp.par.Connect:
				self.setupListeners()
		elif par.name == 'Showdefaultnotechannels':
			self.ownerComp.par.Defaultnoterange1.enable = par.eval()
		elif par.name == 'Showdefaultccchannels':
			self.ownerComp.par.Defaultccrange1.enable = par.eval()
		else:
			TDAbletonCompBaseExt.onParValueChange(self, par, val, prev)

	def checkDeviceEnable(self):
		self.OSCDeviceInCHOP.cook(force=True)
		self.OutCHOP.cook(force=True)

	def setDeviceName(self, name):
		deviceInfo = self.LOMParInfo(self.FinalDevicePar)
		command = deviceInfo['lomExpression'] + '.name = "' + name + '"'
		self.runRemoteCode(command)

	def doMIDICallback(self, address, args):
		channel, info = address.split('/')[-2:]
		channel = int(channel[2:])
		info = info.split('_')
		try:
			typeNumber = info[1]
		except:
			typeNumber = None
		parent().DoCallback("onMIDIEvent",
							{'address': address, 'eventValue': args[0],
							 'channel': channel, 'eventType': info[0],
							 'eventTypeNumber': typeNumber})

	@property
	def deviceOutAddressStripped(self):
		strips = self.ownerComp.par.Stripdeviceprefixsegments.eval()
		address = self.deviceOutAddress[1:]
		for i in range(strips):
			slash = address.find('/')
			if slash == -1:
				address = ''
			else:
				address = address[slash + 1:]
		if address:
			return address + '/'
		else:
			return ''

	@property
	def maxDeviceInfo(self):
		"""
		device par info
		:return: par info or None if invalid device or no device chosen.
		"""
		deviceInfo = self.LOMParInfo(self.FinalDevicePar)
		if deviceInfo and 'ID_TDA_MIDI' in deviceInfo['lomInfo']['aPars']:
			return deviceInfo

*�����������������# me - this DAT
# 
# dat - the DAT that received a message
# rowIndex - the row number the message was placed into
# message - an ascii representation of the data
#           Unprintable characters and unicode characters will
#           not be preserved. Use the 'bytes' parameter to get
#           the raw bytes that were sent.
# bytes - a byte array of the message.
# timeStamp - the arrival time component the OSC message
# address - the address component of the OSC message
# args - a list of values contained within the OSC message
# peer - a Peer object describing the originating message
#   peer.close()    #close the connection
#   peer.owner  #the operator to whom the peer belongs
#   peer.address    #network address associated with the peer
#   peer.port       #network port associated with the peer
#

callbackPar = parent().par.Enablecallbacks
splitNotePar = parent().par.Splittouchingnotes
noteSplitterCHOP = op('noteSplitter')
lastNotePar = parent().par.Lastnotedata
noteDataCHOP = op('noteData')
velocityPar = noteDataCHOP.par.name1
lastNoteSplitterCHOP = op('lastNoteSplitter')

def onReceiveOSC(dat, rowIndex, message, bytes, timeStamp, address, args, peer):
	if callbackPar.eval():
		ext.TDAbletonCompBaseExt.doMIDICallback(address, args)
	if splitNotePar.eval():
		chan = address[1:]
		if args[0] == 0:
			if noteSplitterCHOP[chan] is None:
				noteSplitterCHOP.appendChan(chan)
				run('args[0](args[1], args[2])', destroyNoteSplitChan, 
						noteSplitterCHOP, chan, delayFrames=1, 
						delayRef=op.TDResources)
			if lastNotePar.eval():
				vchan = velocityPar.eval()
				if lastNoteSplitterCHOP[vchan] is None:
					lastNoteSplitterCHOP.appendChan(vchan)
					run('args[0](args[1], args[2])', destroyNoteSplitChan, 
								lastNoteSplitterCHOP, vchan, delayFrames=1, 
								delayRef=op.TDResources)

def destroyNoteSplitChan(chop, name):
	try:
		chop[name].destroy()
	except:
		pass

*�����������������@f# AbletonRackExt

from distutils.log import debug
import socket
from copy import deepcopy

TDF = op.TDModules.mod.TDFunctions
TDAbletonCompBaseExt = \
	mod('abletonBase/TDAbletonCompBaseExt').TDAbletonCompBaseExt


class AbletonRackExt(TDAbletonCompBaseExt):
	"""
	AbletonLevelExt works with TDA_Level Max Device to get audio level data
	"""

	def __init__(self, ownerComp):
		# The component to which this extension is attached
		self.OSCDeviceInCHOP = ownerComp.op('oscin_device')
		self.OSCControlsInDAT = ownerComp.op('oscin_controls')
		self.OSCMacrosSendCHOP = ownerComp.op('macroSends')
		self.ChanNameCHOP = ownerComp.op('chanNames')
		self.InOutIndexes = []
		self.deviceChannel = None
		self.rackName = None
		self.deviceName = None
		self.isRackSet = False
		# can be multiple devices
		TDF.createProperty(self, 'SendDevices', {}, dependable=True)
		TDF.createProperty(self, 'MacroOSCSendAddresses', [], dependable='deep')
		TDAbletonCompBaseExt.__init__(self, ownerComp)

		# list of device parameters to track, by Ableton device par index:
		# 	['TD par name', 'osc_channel_par_name'] or ['name'] or None to skip
		self.devicePars = [['Deviceon', 'Device_On']]
		# info about the rack types. {rack device name: pulse parameter}
		self.midiRackInfo = {'TDA_Instrument_Rack': ownerComp.par.Addir,
							 'TDA_MIDI_Effect_Rack': ownerComp.par.Addmer}
		self.audioRackInfo = {'TDA_Audio_Effect_Rack': ownerComp.par.Addaer}
		self.rackInfo = {}
		self.rackInfo.update(self.midiRackInfo)
		self.rackInfo.update(self.audioRackInfo)
		self.oscDevicePars = [ownerComp.par.Macrososconly,
							  ownerComp.par.Oscinputchop,
							  ownerComp.par.Reorder]
		self.addRackPars = [ownerComp.par.Addaer, ownerComp.par.Addir,
							ownerComp.par.Addmer]

		run('op(' + str(self.ownerComp.id) + \
				').ext.TDAbletonCompBaseExt.checkDeviceEnable()'\
				' if op(' + str(self.ownerComp.id) + ')	else None',
			delayFrames=1, delayRef=op.TDResources)

	def updateLOMPar(self, lomPar, lomInfo):
		"""
		Apply filters to base method.
		Device will be filtered for TDA_Level M4L devices only

		Base method:
		Updates a Live Object Model parameter (Track, Device, Parameter)
		lomPar is the parameter
		lomInfo is the associated dictionary from self.SongInfo

		 menuLabel is LO.name, menuName is LO pointer or
		 	'None' if None
		 	'Not_Found' if  not found
		 if lomInfo is None, set val to 'None' with no other menu options
		"""
		# only include rack devices (not drum pads)
		if lomInfo and (lomPar.name == 'Device'
						or lomPar.name.endswith('device')):
			newLOMInfo = dict()
			for deviceInfo in lomInfo.values():
				if deviceInfo['chainType'] == 'chains':
					newLOMInfo[deviceInfo['name']] = deviceInfo
			# use first available device if current value not found
			label = self.getMenuLabel(lomPar)
			if label.endswith(' (Not Found)'):
				label = label[:-12]
			# if newLOMInfo and (label not in newLOMInfo):
			# 	# hack the valid device name into menu
			# 	labels = lomPar.menuLabels
			# 	labels[lomPar.menuIndex] = list(newLOMInfo.keys())[0]
			# 	lomPar.menuLabels = labels
		else:
			newLOMInfo = lomInfo
		return TDAbletonCompBaseExt.updateLOMPar(self, lomPar, newLOMInfo)

	@property
	def deviceInfo(self):
		"""
		device par info
		:return: par info or None if invalid device or no device chosen.
		"""
		deviceInfo = self.LOMParInfo(self.FinalDevicePar)
		if deviceInfo and deviceInfo['lomInfo']['chainType'] == 'chains':
			return deviceInfo
		else:
			return None

	def setupListeners(self):
		self.isRackSet = False
		self.OSCControlsInDAT.par.active = False
		self.InOutIndexes = []
		self.MacroOSCSendAddresses = []
		self.deviceChannel = None
		TDAbletonCompBaseExt.setupListeners(self)
		oldMacroLabels = []
		# default a bunch of stuff to off, turn it on later if it's valid
		for macroNum in range(1, 9):
			par = getattr(self.ownerComp.par, 'Macro' + str(macroNum))
			oldMacroLabels.append(par.label)
			par.label = 'Macro' + str(macroNum)
			par.enable = False
			par = getattr(self.ownerComp.par, 'Macroosc' + str(macroNum))
			par.enable = False
		for par in self.rackInfo.values():
			par.enable = False
		for par in self.oscDevicePars:
			par.enable = False
		# turn on add rack buttons if we're on a track
		if self.FinalChainPar == 'Track' \
				and self.LOMParInfo(self.FinalChainPar):
			for par in self.audioRackInfo.values():
				par.enable = True
			if self.LOMParInfo(self.FinalChainPar)['lomInfo']['hasMIDIInput']:
				for par in self.midiRackInfo.values():
					par.enable = True

		# check if we have a valid device using deviceInfo property above
		deviceInfo = self.deviceInfo
		if deviceInfo is None:
			self.onDeviceInvalid()
			return
		if ' ' in deviceInfo['lomInfo']['name']:
			self.SetError(
					"TDAbleton Rack Devices can't have spaces in their names",
					label='TDA Rack name')
		else:
			self.SetError('', label='TDA Rack name')

		self.SendDevices.clear()
		self._SendDevices.modified()
		self.isRackSet = True

		# set device networking for all devices in Rack
		rackInfo = deviceInfo['lomInfo']
		maxTrackID = self.MaxTrackID()
		deviceLomExpression = deviceInfo['lomExpression']
		returnAddressBase = deviceInfo['returnAddress']
		sendDeviceInfo = None
		sendDeviceExpr = None
		oscAddressScopes = []

		if rackInfo['chains']:
			chainInfo = next(iter(rackInfo['chains'].values()))
			if chainInfo['hasMIDIOutput']:
				deviceName = 'TDA_Rack_MIDI_OSC'
			else:
				deviceName = 'TDA_Rack_OSC'
			for chain in rackInfo['chains'].values():
				if self.SendDevices:
					break
				for chainDeviceName, device in chain['devices'].items():
					if 'ID_' + deviceName in device['aPars']:
						sendDeviceInfo = device
						sendDeviceExpr = deviceLomExpression + '.' + \
										 chain['expression'] + '.' + \
										 device['expression']
						self.SendDevices[sendDeviceExpr] = sendDeviceInfo
						break

			for expression, device in self.SendDevices.items():
				# device channel name
				self.deviceChannel = \
					'/rack/' + maxTrackID + '/' + rackInfo['name']
				oscAddressScopes.append(self.deviceChannel + '/*')
				self.sendOSC('/tda/command', ['setDeviceNetworking',
											  expression])

		self.OSCDeviceInCHOP.par.oscaddressscope = ' '.join(oscAddressScopes)

		if sendDeviceInfo:
			# other info from device
			ownerId = self.ownerComp.id
			# Parameters (only DeviceOn for rack)
			for aParNum, info in enumerate(self.devicePars[:1]):
				if info is None:
					continue
				if len(info) == 1:
					info.append(info[0])
				par = getattr(self.ownerComp.par, info[0])
				par.enable = True
				returnAddress = returnAddressBase + '/' + info[1] + '/value'
				listener = (deviceLomExpression + '.parameters['
							+ str(aParNum) + ']', 'value', returnAddress,
							ownerId)
				self.addListener(listener, par)

			macroAPars = list(rackInfo['aPars'].keys())
			# we have to search for indexes because names can change
			macroPars = []
			for aParName in macroAPars:
				macroPar = rackInfo['aPars'][aParName]
				if 0 < macroPar['index'] < 9:
					macroPars.append(macroPar)
			# macro controls
			channelNames = [''] * 8
			for macroPar in macroPars:
				index = str(macroPar['index'])
				par = getattr(self.ownerComp.par, 'Macro' + index)
				par.label = macroPar['name']
				self.updateMacroBinds(par,
									  oldMacroLabels[macroPar['index'] - 1])
				channelNames[macroPar['index'] - 1] = macroPar['name']
				par.readOnly = False
				if not self.ownerComp.par.Macrososconly:
					par.enable = True
					# value listener
					lomExpression = deviceLomExpression + '.' \
									+ macroPar['expression']
					valueAddress = returnAddressBase + '/' + \
								   self.validChannelName(macroPar['name']) \
								   + '/value'
					listener = (lomExpression, 'value',
								valueAddress, ownerId)
					self.addListener(listener, par, macroPar['min'],
									 macroPar['max'])
				else:
					par.enable = False
			self.ChanNameCHOP.par.Channelnames = ' '.join(
				[self.ChanNameStripped(self.deviceChannel + '/' +
									   self.validChannelName(name),
									   self.ownerComp.par
									   .Stripdeviceprefixsegments)
				 for name in channelNames])
			for par in self.oscDevicePars:
				par.enable = True
			oscControlAddressScopes = []
			for i in range(1, 9):
				inOutIndex = []
				par = getattr(self.ownerComp.par, 'Macroosc' + str(i))
				par.enable = True
				aPars = [sendDeviceInfo['aPars']['oscIn' + str(i)],
						 sendDeviceInfo['aPars']['oscOut' + str(i)]]
				for aPar in aPars:
					lomExpression = sendDeviceExpr + '.' + \
									aPar['expression']
					valueAddress = returnAddressBase + '/' + aPar['name'] \
								   + '/value'
					listener = (lomExpression, 'value', valueAddress,
								ownerId)
					oscControlAddressScopes.append(valueAddress)
					self.addListener(listener, useBaseOSC=False)
					inOutIndex.append(aPar['index'])
				self.InOutIndexes.append(inOutIndex)
				# Have to delay to prevent feedback loop on startup
				run('op(' + str(self.ownerComp.id) + ').ext.'
					'TDAbletonCompBaseExt.sendMacroOSCInOut(' + str(i) +
					')', delayMilliSeconds=1000, delayRef=op.TDResources)
				# self.sendMacroOSCInOut(i)
				sending = self.MacroOSCInOut(i)[0]
				if sending:
					self.MacroOSCSendAddresses.append(
						self.deviceChannel + '/macro' + str(i))
			self.OSCControlsInDAT.par.addscope = \
				' '.join(oscControlAddressScopes)
			self.OSCControlsInDAT.par.active = \
				self.ownerComp.par.Connect.eval()

		# name
		self.ownerComp.par.Devicename.enable = True
		self.ownerComp.par.Devicename = self.getMenuLabel(
			getattr(self.ownerComp.par, self.FinalDevicePar))

		# oscin ready to go
		self.checkDeviceEnable()
		self.onSendCountChanged()

	def onOutParChange(self, par):
		if par.name.startswith('Macro') and par.readOnly:
			return
		TDAbletonCompBaseExt.onOutParChange(self, par)

	def onDeviceInvalid(self):
		"""
		Call this when we don't have a valid device, to disable stuff
		:return:
		"""
		for info in self.devicePars:
			if info is not None:
				getattr(self.ownerComp.par, info[0]).enable = False
		self.ownerComp.par.Devicename = ''
		self.ownerComp.par.Devicename.enable = False
		self.OSCDeviceInCHOP.par.oscaddressscope = ''
		self.checkDeviceEnable()
		self.OSCControlsInDAT.par.active = False
		self.deviceOutAddress = ''

	def onAbletonNotify(self, info):
		TDAbletonCompBaseExt.onAbletonNotify(self, info)
		# Set device ports when connected
		if info['notificationType'] == 'connected':
			self.checkDeviceEnable()
			self.OSCDeviceInCHOP.par.port = self.TDAbletonComp.par.Inport.eval()
			self.OSCControlsInDAT.par.port = \
				self.TDAbletonComp.par.Inport.eval()
		elif info['notificationType'] == 'disconnected':
			self.checkDeviceEnable()
			self.onSendCountChanged()
		elif info['notificationType'] == 'lomNameChanged':
			deviceInfo = self.deviceInfo
			if not deviceInfo:
				return
			changedObj = info
			devicePtr = deviceInfo['lomInfo']['ptr']
			if changedObj['ptr'] == devicePtr:
				self.ownerComp.par.Devicename = changedObj['name']
				validName = self.validChannelName(changedObj['name'])
				if info['name'] != validName:
					self.setObjectName(validName)
			else:
				# check for rack macro name changes
				try:
					changedObj['parentInfo']['ptr']
				except:
					return
				if changedObj['parentInfo']['ptr'] == devicePtr:
					self.setupListeners()

	def clearOSCIn(self):
		self.OSCDeviceInCHOP.par.resetpulse.pulse()
		TDAbletonCompBaseExt.clearOSCIn(self)

	def onParPulse(self, par):
		if par.name in [p.name for p in self.rackInfo.values()]:
			if not self.TDAbletonComp.Connected:
				return
			for name, p in self.rackInfo.items():
				if p.name == par.name:
					break
			TDAbletonCompBaseExt.addDevice(self, name + '.adg', name)
		else:
			TDAbletonCompBaseExt.onParPulse(self, par)

	def onParValueChange(self, par, val, prev):
		if par.name == 'Devicename':
			# make sure device name is a valid channel name or there'll be
			# problems receiving data
			deviceInfo = self.deviceInfo
			if deviceInfo:
				val = self.validChannelName(val)
				if par.eval() != val or deviceInfo['lomInfo']['name'] != val:
					self.setObjectName(val)
		elif par.name == 'Output':
			self.updateLOMPars()
		elif par.name == 'Connect':
			self.checkDeviceEnable()
			TDAbletonCompBaseExt.onParValueChange(self, par, val, prev)
		elif par.name == 'Stripdeviceprefixsegments':
			self.OSCDeviceInCHOP.par.resetpulse.pulse()
			if self.ownerComp.par.Connect:
				self.setupListeners()
		elif par.name == 'Macrososconly':
			self.setupListeners()
		elif par.name.startswith('Macroosc'):
			self.sendMacroOSCInOut(int(par.name[-1]))
			self.setupListeners()
		else:
			TDAbletonCompBaseExt.onParValueChange(self, par, val, prev)

	def checkDeviceEnable(self):
		self.OutCHOP.cook(force=True)
		self.OSCDeviceInCHOP.cook(force=True)

	def setObjectName(self, name, lomExpression=None):
		"""
		Change device's name
		:param name: new name
		:param lomExpression: expression to object or None to use FinalDevicePar
		:return:
		"""
		if not self.ownerComp.par.Connect:
			return
		if lomExpression is None:
			lomExpression = self.LOMParInfo(
				self.FinalDevicePar)['lomExpression']
		command = lomExpression + '.name = "' + name + '"'
		self.runRemoteCode(command)

	menuIndex2Tuple = {0: [1, 0], 1: [0, 1], 2: [1, 1], 3: [0, 0]}

	def MacroOSCInOut(self, macroNum):
		"""
		Get a list with [in, out] bools for in and out states of OSC device
		:param macroNum: 1-8
		:return: [in, out] bools
		"""
		par = getattr(self.ownerComp.par, 'Macroosc' + str(macroNum))
		return self.menuIndex2Tuple[par.menuIndex]

	def SetMacroOSCInOut(self, macroNum, inOut, value):
		"""
		Set the menu entry properly based on one of the elements
		:param macroNum: 1-9
		:param inOut: 0 for in, 1 for out
		:param value: boolean
		"""
		currentValue = self.MacroOSCInOut(macroNum)
		newValue = currentValue.copy()
		newValue[inOut] = value
		if newValue != currentValue:
			par = getattr(self.ownerComp.par, 'Macroosc' + str(macroNum))
			for index, pair in self.menuIndex2Tuple.items():
				if newValue == pair:
					par.menuIndex = index
					return

	def onSendCountChanged(self):
		if self.deviceChannel:
			offMacros = ['1', '2', '3', '4', '5', '6', '7', '8']
			if self.ownerComp.par.Connect and self.TDAbletonComp.Connected:
				for chan in self.OSCMacrosSendCHOP.chans():
					macroNum = chan.name[-1]
					macroAddress = self.deviceChannel + '/control' + macroNum
					self.TDAbletonComp.SendMaxOSC(macroAddress, [1])
					debug(chan.name)
					if macroNum in offMacros:
						offMacros.remove(macroNum)
					macroPar = getattr(self.ownerComp.par, 'Macro' + macroNum)
					macroPar.readOnly = True
			for macroNum in offMacros:
				macroAddress = self.deviceChannel + '/control' + macroNum
				self.TDAbletonComp.SendMaxOSC(macroAddress, [0])
				macroPar = getattr(self.ownerComp.par, 'Macro' + macroNum)
				macroPar.readOnly = False

	def sendMacroOSCInOut(self, macroNum):
		"""
		Send the values from Component Macro OSC parameter to Live
		:param macroNum: 1-8
		"""
		if not self.ownerComp.par.Connect:
			return

		settings = self.MacroOSCInOut(macroNum)
		sendDeviceExpr, sendDevice = next(iter(self.SendDevices.items()))
		for inOut in range(2):
			command = sendDeviceExpr + '.parameters[' \
					  + str(self.InOutIndexes[macroNum - 1][inOut]) \
					  + '].value = ' + str(settings[inOut])
			self.runRemoteCode(command)

	def onControlsChange(self, address, args):
		macroNum = int(address[-7])
		inOut = 0 if address[-8] == 'n' else 1
		self.SetMacroOSCInOut(macroNum, inOut, args[0])

	@property
	def deviceOutAddressStripped(self):
		strips = self.ownerComp.par.Stripdeviceprefixsegments.eval()
		address = self.deviceOutAddress[1:]
		for i in range(strips):
			slash = address.find('/')
			if slash == -1:
				address = ''
			else:
				address = address[slash + 1:]
		if address:
			return address + '/'
		else:
			return ''

	def updateMacroBinds(self, par, oldLabel):
		if oldLabel == par.label:
			return
		bindChain = TDF.bindChain(par, parsOnly=True)
		done = set()
		def updateBindReferences(targetPar):
			if targetPar in done:
				return
			done.add(targetPar)
			if targetPar.label == oldLabel:
				try:
					targetPar.label = par.label
				except:
					# builtin parameter or some other nonsense
					pass
			for bindRef in targetPar.bindReferences:
				if isinstance(bindRef, Par):
					updateBindReferences(bindRef)

		updateBindReferences(bindChain[-1])

*�����������������/}# AbletonSongExt

TDF = op.TDModules.mod.TDFunctions

TDAbletonCompBaseExt = \
	mod('abletonBase/TDAbletonCompBaseExt').TDAbletonCompBaseExt


class AbletonSongExt(TDAbletonCompBaseExt):
	"""
	AbletonParameterExt sets up a 2 way connection with an Ableton track.
	"""

	def __init__(self, ownerComp):
		# The component to which this extension is attached
		TDAbletonCompBaseExt.__init__(self, ownerComp)
		self.OSCSceneInDAT = ownerComp.op('oscin_scene')
		self.cuePointCountDAT = ownerComp.op('cuePointCount')
		self.scenesCountDAT = ownerComp.op('scenesCount')
		self.cuePointTable = ownerComp.op('cuePointTable')
		self.outCHOP = ownerComp.op('out1')
		self.timeCHOP = parent().time # for syncing
		self.playListener = None
		TDF.createProperty(self, 'HasMaster', value=False, readOnly=True,
						   dependable=True)
		TDF.createProperty(self, 'LastStartedScene', value=-1, readOnly=True,
						   dependable=True)
		TDF.createProperty(self, 'LastCuePointPassed', value=-1, readOnly=True,
						   dependable=True)
		TDF.createProperty(self, 'CuePoints', value=[], readOnly=True,
						   dependable=True)

	def setupListeners(self):
		run("args[0].TDAbletonComp.SendMaxOSC('/updateTime', [1])",
			self, endFrame=True, delayRef=op.TDResources)
		TDAbletonCompBaseExt.setupListeners(self)

		# liveExpression: Live API Python expression to object
		# returnAddress: osc address that updates will be sent to
		# id: unique id of the object requesting a listener
		# property: the name of the live object's property to listen to
		ownerId = self.ownerComp.id
		returnAddress = '/song/info'

		# if self.ownerComp.par.Timedata.eval():
		# 	liveExpression = '*BeatTime' # special liveExpressions start with *
		# 	listener = (liveExpression, '', returnAddress, ownerId)
		# 	self.addListener(listener)

		listener = ('SONG', 'is_playing', returnAddress + '/play', ownerId)
		self.playListener = listener
		self.addListener(listener)
		# listener = ('SONG', 'last_event_time', returnAddress + '/last_event_time', ownerId)
		# self.playListener = listener
		# self.addListener(listener)
		listener = ('SONG', 'song_length',
					returnAddress + '/song_length', ownerId)
		self.addListener(listener)
		for property in ['loop', 'loop_start', 'loop_length', 
						 'record_mode', 'nudge_up', 'nudge_down']:
			listener = ('SONG', property,
						returnAddress + '/' + property, ownerId)
			parName = property.replace('_', '').capitalize()
			self.addListener(listener, getattr(self.ownerComp.par, parName))
		self.addListener( 	('SONG', 'signature_numerator', 
							returnAddress + '/signature_numerator', 
							ownerId), self.ownerComp.par.Signature1)
		self.addListener( 	('SONG', 'signature_denominator', 
							returnAddress + '/signature_denominator', 
							ownerId), self.ownerComp.par.Signature2)
		# master device
		self._HasMaster.val = bool(self.TDAbletonComp.TDAMasterDevice)
		if self.HasMaster:
			deviceInfo = self.TDAbletonComp.TDAMasterDevice
			lomExpression = 'SONG.master_track.' + deviceInfo['expression'] + \
							'.' + deviceInfo['aPars']['songID']['expression']
			listener = (lomExpression, 'value', returnAddress + '/song_id',
						ownerId)
			self.addListener(listener, self.ownerComp.par.Songid)
			# listener = (lomExpression, 'name', '/info/listener' +
			# 			returnAddress + '/song_name', ownerId)
			# self.addListener(listener)
			self.ownerComp.par.Devicesongname.enable = True
			self.ownerComp.par.Devicesongname = deviceInfo['name']
			self.ownerComp.par.Songid.enable = True
			self.ownerComp.par.Addtdamasterdevice.enable = False

			self.sendOSC('/tda/command', ['setDeviceNetworking',
										  'SONG.master_track.' + deviceInfo[
											  'expression']])
		else:
			self.ownerComp.par.Devicesongname = ''
			self.ownerComp.par.Devicesongname.enable = False
			self.ownerComp.par.Songid = 0
			self.ownerComp.par.Songid.enable = False
			self.ownerComp.par.Addtdamasterdevice.enable = True

		self.OSCInDAT.par.addscope = '/listener/song/info/*'
		self.OSCInCHOP.par.oscaddressscope = '/song/info/*'

	def onAbletonNotify(self, info):
		TDAbletonCompBaseExt.onAbletonNotify(self, info)
		# Set midi ports when connected
		if info['notificationType'] in ['songInfo', 'connected']:
			self.cuePointCountDAT.cook(force=True)
			if info['notificationType'] == 'connected':
				self._LastStartedScene.val = -1
				self.OSCSceneInDAT.par.port = \
					self.TDAbletonComp.par.Inport.eval()
				self.scenesCountDAT.cook(force=True)
				ext.CallbacksExt.DoCallback('onTDAConnectionChange',
											{'connected': True})
			else:
				ext.CallbacksExt.DoCallback('onSongInfoChanged')
			self.updateCuePoints()
		elif info['notificationType'] == 'disconnected':
			ext.CallbacksExt.DoCallback('onTDAConnectionChange',
										{'connected': False})
		elif info['notificationType'] in ['lomNameChanged',
										  'cuePointTimeChanged']:
			# brute force... could check to see if it's a cuePoint
			changedObj = info
			if self.TDAbletonComp.ObjectInfoByPtr[changedObj['ptr']]['byPtr'] \
					['expression'].startswith('cue_points'):
				self.updateCuePoints()
				self.cuePointCountDAT.cook(force=True)
				run('args[0].checkCuePoints()', self, delayFrames=1)
			elif self.TDAbletonComp.ObjectInfoByPtr[changedObj['ptr']]['byPtr'] \
					['expression'].startswith('scenes'):
				self.scenesCountDAT.cook(force=True)
			elif info['notificationType'] == 'lomNameChanged':
				if changedObj == self.TDAbletonComp.TDAMasterDevice:
					self.ownerComp.par.Devicesongname = changedObj['name']
				ext.CallbacksExt.DoCallback('onSongInfoChanged')

	def updateCuePoints(self):
		self._CuePoints.val = list(self.SongInfo['cuePoints'].values())
		self._CuePoints.modified()
		cuePointsSorted = list(self.SongInfo['cuePoints'].values())
		cuePointsSorted.sort(key=lambda x: x['time'])
		cuePointNames = [c['name'] for c in cuePointsSorted]
		self.ownerComp.par.Cuepointtojumpto.menuNames = cuePointNames
		self.ownerComp.par.Cuepointtojumpto.menuLabels = cuePointNames
		# self.ownerComp.par.Synctimelinetocuepoint.menuNames = ['Off'] + \
		# 													  cuePointNames
		# self.ownerComp.par.Synctimelinetocuepoint.menuLabels = ['Off'] + \
		# 													   cuePointNames

	def onSceneTriggered(self, scene):
		"""
		callback from OSCSceneInDAT

		we do it this way to make absolutely sure we don't miss a sceneTrigger
		message. CHOPs can miss changes due to missed frames etc.
		"""
		if scene != -1:
			sceneInfo = self.SongInfo['scenes'][scene]
			ext.CallbacksExt.DoCallback('onSceneTriggered', {'sceneNum': scene,
						'sceneName': sceneInfo['name'],'sceneInfo': sceneInfo})

	def onSceneStarted(self, scene):
		self._LastStartedScene.val = scene
		sceneInfo = self.SongInfo['scenes'][scene]
		ext.CallbacksExt.DoCallback('onSceneStarted', {'sceneNum': scene,
						'sceneName': sceneInfo['name'], 'sceneInfo': sceneInfo})
	def onCuePointPassed(self, index):
		index += 1
		cuePointIndex = self.CuePointIndex(index)
		if index - 1 >= len(self.CuePoints) or cuePointIndex is None:
			return
		self._LastCuePointPassed.val = index
		ext.CallbacksExt.DoCallback('onCuePointPassed',
									{'cuePointNum': cuePointIndex,
									 'cuePoint': self.CuePoints[cuePointIndex]})

	def onCuePointChanged(self, index):
		cuePointIndex = self.CuePointIndex(index)
		if index - 1 >= len(self.CuePoints):
			return
		if cuePointIndex is None:
			ext.CallbacksExt.DoCallback('onCuePointChanged',
									{'cuePointNum':None,
									 'cuePoint': None})		
		else:		
			ext.CallbacksExt.DoCallback('onCuePointChanged',
									{'cuePointNum':index,
									 'cuePoint': self.CuePoints[cuePointIndex]
								 					if index != 0 else None})

	def CuePointIndex(self, index):
		"""
		Get the internal index (for Ableton and CuePoints list) from the
		last_cuepoint_passed and current_cuepoint channel values or the
		cuePoints table output row #

		:param index: the index from CHOP channels or cuePoints output row #
		:return: internal CuePoint index or None if invalid index (e.g. 0)
		"""
		cell = self.cuePointTable[str(index - 1), 0]
		if cell is None:
			return None
		else:
			return cell.row

	def GetCuePointByName(self, name):
		cuePointsSorted = list(self.SongInfo['cuePoints'].values())
		cuePointsSorted.sort(key=lambda x: x['time'])
		for c in cuePointsSorted:
			if c['name'] == name:
				return c

	def JumpToCuePoint(self, cuePoint):
		if isinstance(cuePoint, str):
			cuePoint = self.GetCuePointByName(cuePoint)['index']
		self.sendOSC('/shell/runCode',[
					 		'SONG.cue_points[' + str(cuePoint) + '].jump()'])

	def onLoopStartPassed(self):
		ext.CallbacksExt.DoCallback('onLoopStartPassed')

	def SceneInfoByIndex(self, index):
		"""
		Get scene info dict by index.
		"""
		if -1 < index < len(self.SongInfo['scenes']):
			return self.SongInfo['scenes'][index]
		else:
			return None

	def SceneInfoByName(self, name):
		"""
		Get scene info dict by name
		"""
		for sceneInfo in self.SongInfo['scenes']:
			if sceneInfo['name'] == 'name':
				return sceneInfo
		return None

	def onParValueChange(self, par, val, prev):
		if par.name == 'Devicesongname':
			if self.TDAbletonComp.TDAMasterDevice and \
					self.TDAbletonComp.TDAMasterDevice['name'] != par.eval():
				self.setDeviceName(val)
		elif par.name == 'Timedata':
			if self.Connected:
				self.setupListeners()
		else:
			TDAbletonCompBaseExt.onParValueChange(self, par, val, prev)

	def onParPulse(self, par):
		if par.name in ['Play', 'Stop']:
			if self.playListener and self.Connected:
				if par.name == 'Play':
					self.setListener(self.playListener, 1)
				if par.name == 'Stop':
					self.setListener(self.playListener, 0)
		elif par.name == 'Addtdamasterdevice':
			self.TDAbletonComp.AddTDAMaxDevice('SONG.master_track',
											   'TDA_Master.amxd', 'TD Song')
		elif par.name == 'Firescene':
			self.FireScene()
		elif par.name == 'Stopallclips':
			self.StopAllClips()
		elif par.name == 'Jump':
			self.JumpToCuePoint(self.ownerComp.par.Cuepointtojumpto.eval())
		elif par.name == 'Tempotap':
			if par.eval():
				self.sendOSC('/shell/runCode', ['SONG.tap_tempo()'])
		else:
			TDAbletonCompBaseExt.onParPulse(self, par)

	def setDeviceName(self, name):
		deviceInfo = self.TDAbletonComp.TDAMasterDevice
		lomExpression = 'SONG.master_track.' + deviceInfo['expression']
		command = lomExpression + '.name = "' + name + '"'
		self.runRemoteCode(command)

	def StopAllClips(self):
		"""
		Stop all clips in song
		"""
		self.runRemoteCode('SONG.stop_all_clips()')

	def FireScene(self, scene=None):
		"""
		Fire a scene.
		:param scene: if int, number of the scene to fire. If str, name of scene
			to fire. If None, scene selected in Scene To Fire parameter
		"""
		if scene is None:
			sceneNum = int(self.ownerComp.par.Scenetofire.eval()[5:])
		else:
			menuLabels = self.ownerComp.par.Scenetofire.menuLabels
			if isinstance(scene, int):
				if scene >= len(self.SongInfo['scenes']) or scene < 0:
					print(self.ownerComp.path,
						  'FireScene invalid scene number:', scene)
					return
				sceneNum = scene
			elif scene in menuLabels:
				sceneNum = menuLabels.index(scene)
			else:
				print(self.ownerComp.path, 'FireScene invalid scene name:',
					  scene)
				return
		self.runRemoteCode('SONG.scenes[' + str(sceneNum) + '].fire()')

	def checkCuePoints(self):
		time = self.outCHOP['song/info/time'].eval()
		for cuePoint in self.CuePoints:
			if time == cuePoint['time']:
				self.onCuePointPassed(cuePoint['index'])

	def TimelineSyncLength(self):
		timelineMinutes = self.outCHOP.chan('*song_length')[0] / \
							self.outCHOP.chan('*tempo')[0]
		self.timeCHOP.tempo = self.outCHOP.chan('*tempo')[0]
		self.timeCHOP.start = self.timeCHOP.rangeStart = 1
		self.timeCHOP.end = self.timeCHOP.rangeEnd = \
							self.timeCHOP.rate * timelineMinutes * 60

	def TimelineSyncPlay(self):
		self.timeCHOP.par.play = self.outCHOP.chan('*play')[0]

	def TimelineSyncPos(self):
		posMinutes = self.outCHOP.chan('*time')[0] / \
							self.outCHOP.chan('*tempo')[0]
		self.timeCHOP.frame = self.timeCHOP.rate * posMinutes *60

	def TimelineSyncTempo(self):
		self.timeCHOP.tempo = self.outCHOP.chan('*tempo')[0]

*�����������������6# me - this DAT
# 
# dat - the DAT that received a message
# rowIndex - the row number the message was placed into
# message - an ascii representation of the data
#           Unprintable characters and unicode characters will
#           not be preserved. Use the 'bytes' parameter to get
#           the raw bytes that were sent.
# bytes - a byte array of the message.
# timeStamp - the arrival time component the OSC message
# address - the address component of the OSC message
# args - a list of values contained within the OSC message
# peer - a Peer object describing the originating message
#   peer.close()    #close the connection
#   peer.owner  #the operator to whom the peer belongs
#   peer.address    #network address associated with the peer
#   peer.port       #network port associated with the peer
#

def onReceiveOSC(dat, rowIndex, message, bytes, timeStamp, address, args, peer):
	if address.endswith('triggered_scene'):
		ext.AbletonSongExt.onSceneTriggered(args[0])
	elif address.endswith('started_scene'):
		ext.AbletonSongExt.onSceneStarted(args[0])

*�����������������00#AbletonTrackExt

TDF = op.TDModules.mod.TDFunctions

TDAbletonCompBaseExt = \
					mod('abletonBase/TDAbletonCompBaseExt').TDAbletonCompBaseExt

CallbacksExt = mod('CallbacksExt').CallbacksExt

class AbletonTrackExt(CallbacksExt, TDAbletonCompBaseExt):
	"""
	AbletonTrackExt sets up a 2 way connection with an Ableton track.
	"""
	def __init__(self, ownerComp):
		# The component to which this extension is attached
		CallbacksExt.__init__(self, ownerComp)
		TDAbletonCompBaseExt.__init__(self, ownerComp)
		self.mergeCHOP = self.ownerComp.op('merge_addDefaults')
		self.clipStatusDAT = self.ownerComp.op('merge_statusCol')

		TDF.createProperty(self, 'ReturnAddress', value='', readOnly=True,
						   									dependable=True)
		# clip stuff
		TDF.createProperty(self, 'ClipID', value='_', readOnly=True,
						   									dependable=True)
		self.clipListeners = []
		self.ClipsTable = self.ownerComp.op('clipsTable')
		self.clipProps = ['playing_position', 'start_marker', 'end_marker',
					 		'loop_start', 'loop_end']
		self.clipListeners = [] # clip listener keys
		TDF.createProperty(self, 'ClipExpression', value='',
						readOnly=True, dependable=True)
		TDF.createProperty(self, 'PlayingSlotIndex', value=-1,
						readOnly=True, dependable=True)
		TDF.createProperty(self, 'FiredSlotIndex', value=-1,
						readOnly=True, dependable=True)

		TDF.createProperty(self, 'ClipSlots', value=[], readOnly=True,
						   									dependable=True)
		TDF.createProperty(self, 'WatchClips', value=False, readOnly=True,
						   									dependable=True)
		self._WatchClips.val = hasattr(self.ownerComp.par, 'Includeclipdata') \
							   and self.ownerComp.par.Includeclipdata.eval()
		# store these for when we need to do stuff with clips etc.
		self.lomExpression = ''
		# key for color listener (needed to set color)
		self.colorListener = None
		

	def setupListeners(self):
		# grab index before clear

		self.colorListener = None
		self.onColorChange()
		self.onClipColorChange()
		# make sure we have a valid track
		chainInfo = self.LOMParInfo(self.FinalChainPar)
		if not chainInfo:
			for parName in ['Mute', 'Solo', 'Crossfader', 'Cuevolume',
							'Panning', 'Trackactivator', 'Volume', 'Colorr']:
				getattr(self.ownerComp.par, parName).enable = False
			self._ClipSlots.val = []
			self.disconnect()
			self._ReturnAddress.val = ''
			return

		oldReturnAddress = self.ReturnAddress

		# returnAddress: osc address that updates will be sent to from tda.
		# note that this must be a valid CHOP channel name
		self._ReturnAddress.val = returnAddress = chainInfo['returnAddress']

		if self.mergeCHOP:
			# do this before channels get wiped
			clipIndexChannel = self.mergeCHOP.chan('*playing_slot_index')
			if clipIndexChannel is not None and \
											oldReturnAddress == returnAddress:
				index = int(clipIndexChannel.eval())
			else:
				index = -1

		TDAbletonCompBaseExt.setupListeners(self)

		# use this to figure out what kind of chain we're dealing with
		chainExpression = chainInfo['lomInfo']['expression']

		# set up listener parameters
		# lomExpression: a Python expression to the live object in tda
		self.lomExpression = lomExpression = chainInfo['lomExpression']

		# id: unique id of the object requesting a listener
		ownerId = self.ownerComp.id
		# read only properties
		readOnlyProps = []

		# tracks
		if self.FinalChainPar == 'Track' \
					and self.ownerComp.par.Includeaudiodata.eval() \
					and not chainInfo['lomInfo']['hasMIDIOutput']:
			readOnlyProps = ['output_meter_left', 'output_meter_right',
							 'output_meter_level']
		# audio and midi tracks
		if chainExpression.startswith('track') and \
								hasattr(self.ownerComp.par, 'Includeclipdata'):
			readOnlyProps += ['playing_slot_index', 'fired_slot_index']
			self.clipListeners = [] # was already cleared in base setupListeners
			self._setClipSlots()
			self.setClipListeners(index)
			self._WatchClips.val = self.ownerComp.par.Includeclipdata.eval()
		else:
			self._ClipSlots.val = []
			self._WatchClips.val = False

		for prop in readOnlyProps:
			listener = (lomExpression, prop, returnAddress + '/' + prop,
						ownerId)
			self.addListener(listener, None)

		self.ownerComp.par.Colorr.enable = True
		self.colorListener = (lomExpression, 'color', 
								returnAddress + '/chain_color', ownerId)
		self.addListener(self.colorListener, None)

		for property in  ['mute', 'solo']:
			par = getattr(self.ownerComp.par, property.capitalize())
			if chainExpression.startswith('master_track'):
				par.enable = False
			else:
				listener = (lomExpression, property,
									returnAddress + '/' + property, ownerId)
				par.enable = True
				self.addListener(listener, par)

		# mixer stuff
		device = chainInfo['lomInfo']['devices']['# Mixer #']
		lomExpression += '.mixer_device.'
		for mixerProp in ['Crossfader', 'Cue Volume', 'Panning',
						  						'Track Activator', 'Volume']:
			parName = mixerProp.replace(' ', '').capitalize()
			returnAddressName = mixerProp.replace(' ', '_')
			par = getattr(self.ownerComp.par, parName)
			if mixerProp not in device['aPars']:
				par.enable = False
				par.val = 0
				continue
			par.enable = True
			propInfo = device['aPars'][mixerProp]
			mixerExpression = lomExpression + propInfo['expression']
			mixerAddress = returnAddress + '/__Mixer__/' + \
						   					returnAddressName + '/value'
			self.addListener((mixerExpression, 'value', mixerAddress, ownerId),
								par, propInfo['min'], propInfo['max'])

	def onParValueChange(self, par, val, prev):
		if par.name in ['Includeaudiodata', 'Includeclipdata'] \
															and self.Connected:
			self.setupListeners()
			self.clearOSCIn()
		elif par.name.startswith('Color'):
			if self.colorListener in self.Listeners:
				i = self.ConvertRGBToInt(
						self.ownerComp.par.Colorr.eval(),
						self.ownerComp.par.Colorg.eval(),
						self.ownerComp.par.Colorb.eval()
				)
				self.setListener(self.colorListener, i, ignoreErrors=True)
				#self.onColorChange()
		elif par.name.startswith('Clipcolor'):
			if par.enable:
				i = self.ConvertRGBToInt(
						self.ownerComp.par.Clipcolorr.eval(),
						self.ownerComp.par.Clipcolorg.eval(),
						self.ownerComp.par.Clipcolorb.eval()
				)
				self.runRemoteCode(self.ClipExpression + '.color = ' + str(i))
		else:
			TDAbletonCompBaseExt.onParValueChange(self, par, val, prev)

	def onParPulse(self, par):
		if par.name == 'Stopallclips':
			self.StopAllClips()
		else:
			TDAbletonCompBaseExt.onParPulse(self, par)

	def onOutputsChannelInit(self, channel, value):	
		if channel.endswith('chain_color'):
			self.onColorChange()
		elif channel.endswith('/color'):
			self.onClipColorChange()
		else:
			TDAbletonCompBaseExt.onOutputsChannelInit(self, channel, value)	

	def onColorChange(self, chanName='*chain_color', parName='Color'):
		try:
			val = self.OutCHOP.chans(chanName)[0].eval()
		except:
			val = 0
			self.ownerComp.par[f'{parName}r'].enable = False
		else:
			if val == -1:
				val = 0
				self.ownerComp.par[f'{parName}r'].enable = False
		self.ownerComp.par[f'{parName}r'], self.ownerComp.par[f'{parName}g'], \
				self.ownerComp.par[f'{parName}b'] = \
												self.ConvertIntToRGB(int(val))

	def onClipColorChange(self):
		if self.ownerComp.par['Clipcolorr'] is None:
			return
		self.onColorChange('*/color', 'Clipcolor')

	def onAbletonNotify(self, info):
		TDAbletonCompBaseExt.onAbletonNotify(self, info)
		# Set midi ports when connected
		if info['notificationType'] in ['clipSlotChanged']:
			try:
				trackPtr = int(self.ownerComp.par.Track.eval())
			except:
				pass
			else:
				if info['parentInfo']['ptr'] == trackPtr:
					self._clipSlotChanged(info)
		# elif info['notificationType'] == 'connected':
		# 	self.setClipListeners()

	def _clipSlotChanged(self, info):
		self._ClipSlots.modified()

	def _setClipSlots(self):
		self._ClipSlots.val = self.LOMParInfo('Track')['lomInfo']['clipSlots']

	def setClipListeners(self, index=None):
		if not self.Connected:
			self.onClipColorChange()
			return
		# remove old listeners
		for listener in self.clipListeners:
			if listener in self.Listeners:
				self.removeListener(listener)
		# figure out clip expression
		if index is None:
			index = self.PlayingSlotIndex
		if self.ClipID != str(index):
			for name in [c.name for c in self.OSCInCHOP.chans('*__clip__*')]:
				self.OSCInCHOP[name].destroy()	
			self._ClipID.val = str(index) if index >= 0 else '_'
		if index >= 0 and self.ClipSlots[index]['clip']:
			clipExpression = self.lomExpression + '.clip_slots[' + \
							 							str(index) + '].clip'
		else:
			clipExpression = ''
		self._ClipExpression.val = clipExpression
		if clipExpression and index >= 0:
			self.ownerComp.par.Clipcolorr.enable = True		
			if self.ownerComp.par.Includeclipdata.eval():
				self.clipListeners = []
				for prop in self.clipProps:
					par = getattr(self.ownerComp.par,
								prop.replace('_', '').capitalize(), None)
					listener = (clipExpression, prop,
							self.ReturnAddress + f'/__clip__/{index}/' + prop,
							self.ownerComp.id)
					self.clipListeners.append(listener)
					if par is not None:
						self.addListener(listener, par)
						par.enable = True
					else:
						self.addListener(listener)
		else:
			try:
				self.OSCInCHOP.chans('*/color')[0].destroy()
			except:
				pass
			self.ownerComp.par.Clipcolorr.enable = False		
			for prop in self.clipProps:
				par = getattr(self.ownerComp.par,
							prop.replace('_', '').capitalize(), None)
				if par is not None:
					par.enable = False
			self.clipListeners = []
		self.onClipColorChange()

	def onClipStatusChange(self):
		try:
			playingSlot = int(self.mergeCHOP.chan('*playing_slot_index'))

		except:
			playingSlot = -1
		try:
			firedSlot = int(self.mergeCHOP.chan('*fired_slot_index'))
		except:
			firedSlot = -1

		# callbacks
		if playingSlot != self.PlayingSlotIndex:
			doCallback = self.ownerComp.par.Enablecallbacks and \
						 not(playingSlot < 0 and self.PlayingSlotIndex < 0)
			self._PlayingSlotIndex.val = playingSlot
			self.setClipListeners()
			if doCallback:
				try:
					clipInfo = self.ClipSlots[playingSlot]['clip'] \
													if playingSlot >=0 else None
				except:
					clipInfo = None
				if clipInfo:
					infoDict = {'name': clipInfo['name'],
								'filePath': clipInfo['filePath'],
								'color': clipInfo['color'],
								'index': playingSlot}
				else:
					infoDict = {'name': None,
								'filePath': None,
								'color': None,
								'index': playingSlot}
				self.DoCallback('onPlayingClipChanged', infoDict)
		if firedSlot != self.FiredSlotIndex:
			doCallback = self.ownerComp.par.Enablecallbacks and \
						 not(firedSlot < 0 and self.FiredSlotIndex < 0)
			self._FiredSlotIndex.val = firedSlot
			if doCallback:
				try:
					clipInfo = self.ClipSlots[firedSlot]['clip'] \
													if firedSlot >=0 else None
				except:
					clipInfo = None
				if clipInfo:
					infoDict = {'name': clipInfo['name'],
								'filePath': clipInfo['filePath'],
								'color': clipInfo['color'],
								'index': firedSlot}
				else:
					infoDict = {'name': None,
								'filePath': None,
								'color': None,
								'index': firedSlot}
				self.DoCallback('onFiredClipChanged', infoDict)


	def FireClipSlot(self, clipSlot):
		"""
		Fire (trigger) the clip slot at index clipSlot
		"""
		if clipSlot < len(self.ClipSlots) and self.lomExpression:
			self.runRemoteCode(self.lomExpression +
							   '.clip_slots[' + str(clipSlot) + '].fire()')
		else:
			debug("FireClipSlot - Invalid clip slot:", clipSlot)

	def StopClipSlot(self, clipSlot):
		"""
		Stop the clip slot at index clipSlot
		"""
		if clipSlot < len(self.ClipSlots) and self.lomExpression:
			self.runRemoteCode(self.lomExpression +
							   '.clip_slots[' + str(clipSlot) + '].stop()')
		else:
			debug("FireClipSlot - Invalid clip slot:", clipSlot)

	def StopAllClips(self):
		"""
		Stop all clips from playing
		"""
		if self.lomExpression:
			self.runRemoteCode(self.lomExpression + '.stop_all_clips()')

	@property
	def ClipPrefix(self):
		try:
			return self.ChanNameStripped(self.ReturnAddress + '/__clip__/') + \
					self.ClipID + '/'
		except:
			return ''

	@property
	def ClipColor(self):
		cell = self.ClipsTable[self.ClipID, 4]
		if cell is None or cell.val == '':
			return -1
		else:
			return cell.val

*�����������������1k
# AbletonLevelExt

import socket
from copy import deepcopy
TDF = op.TDModules.mod.TDFunctions
TDAbletonCompBaseExt = \
					mod('abletonBase/TDAbletonCompBaseExt').TDAbletonCompBaseExt

class AbletonLevelExt(TDAbletonCompBaseExt):
	"""
	AbletonLevelExt works with TDA_Level Max Device to get audio level data
	"""
	def __init__(self, ownerComp):
		# The component to which this extension is attached
		self.OSCDeviceInCHOP = ownerComp.op('oscin_device')
		self.ChanNameCHOP = ownerComp.op('chanNames')
		self.deviceName = 'TDA_Level'
		self.rackName = 'TDA_Audio_Analyzer'
		self.isRackSet = False
		# can be multiple devices in rack mode ['expression':device]
		TDF.createProperty(self, 'SendDevices', dict(), dependable=True)
		TDAbletonCompBaseExt.__init__(self, ownerComp)

		# list of device parameters to track, by Ableton device par index:
		# 	['TD par name', 'osc_channel_par_name'] or ['name'] or None to skip
		self.devicePars = [['Deviceon', 'Device_On'],
					  		['Mix']]

		run('args[0].ext.AbletonLevelExt.checkDeviceEnable()'\
				' if args[0] else None',
					self.ownerComp, delayFrames=1, delayRef=op.TDResources)

	def updateLOMPar(self, lomPar, lomInfo):
		"""
		Apply filters to base method.
		Device will be filtered for TDA_Level M4L devices only

		Base method:
		Updates a Live Object Model parameter (Track, Device, Parameter)
		lomPar is the parameter
		lomInfo is the associated dictionary from self.SongInfo

		 menuLabel is LO.name, menuName is LO pointer or
		 	'None' if None
		 	'Not_Found' if  not found
		 if lomInfo is None, set val to 'None' with no other menu options
		"""
		# only include TDA_Level devices if specific device mode
		# 	or rack devices if rack mode
		if lomInfo and (lomPar.name == 'Device'
						or lomPar.name.endswith('device')):
			newLOMInfo = dict()
			for deviceInfo in lomInfo.values():
				if (self.ownerComp.par.Output == 'Specific'
					and 'ID_' + self.deviceName in deviceInfo['aPars']) \
								or deviceInfo['chainType']:
					newLOMInfo[deviceInfo['name']] = deviceInfo
			# use first available device if current value not found
			label = self.getMenuLabel(lomPar)
			if label.endswith(' (Not Found)'):
				label = label[:-12]
			# if newLOMInfo and (label not in newLOMInfo):
			# 	# hack the valid device name into menu
			# 	labels = lomPar.menuLabels
			# 	labels[lomPar.menuIndex] = list(newLOMInfo.keys())[0]
			# 	lomPar.menuLabels = labels
		else:
			newLOMInfo = lomInfo
		return TDAbletonCompBaseExt.updateLOMPar(self, lomPar, newLOMInfo)

	@property
	def deviceInfo(self):
		"""
		device par info
		:return: par info or None if invalid device or no device chosen.
		"""
		deviceInfo = self.LOMParInfo(self.FinalDevicePar)
		if deviceInfo and \
				((self.ownerComp.par.Output == 'Specific' and \
					'ID_' + self.deviceName in deviceInfo['lomInfo']['aPars'])\
				or (self.ownerComp.par.Output == 'Rack' and \
					deviceInfo['lomInfo']['chainType'])):
			return deviceInfo
		else:
			return None

	def setupListeners(self):
		self.isRackSet = False
		TDAbletonCompBaseExt.setupListeners(self)

		self.ownerComp.par.Adddevice.enable = False
		self.ownerComp.par.Addrack.enable = False
		self.ChanNameCHOP.par.Channelnames = ''
		oldMacroLabels = []
		for macroNum in range(1, 9):
			par = getattr(self.ownerComp.par, 'Macro' + str(macroNum))
			oldMacroLabels.append(par.label)
			par.label = 'Macro' + str(macroNum)
			par.enable = False
		if self.LOMParInfo(self.FinalChainPar) \
											and self.FinalChainPar == 'Track':
			if self.ownerComp.par.Output == 'Specific':
				self.ownerComp.par.Adddevice.enable = True
			else:
				self.ownerComp.par.Addrack.enable = True

		# check if we have a valid device using deviceInfo property above
		deviceInfo = self.deviceInfo
		if deviceInfo is None:
			self.onDeviceInvalid()
			return

		self.SendDevices.clear()
		self._SendDevices.modified()
		maxTrackID = self.MaxTrackID()
		if self.ownerComp.par.Output == 'Rack':
			# set device networking for all level devices in Rack
			self.isRackSet = True
			rackInfo = deviceInfo['lomInfo']
			deviceLomExpression = deviceInfo['lomExpression']
			returnAddressBase = deviceInfo['returnAddress']

			for chain in rackInfo['chains'].values():
				for deviceName, device in chain['devices'].items():
					if 'ID_' + self.deviceName in device['aPars']:
						self.SendDevices[chain['expression'] + '.' +
										device['expression']] = device

			oscAddressScopes = []

			for expression, device in self.SendDevices.items():
				# device channel name
				deviceChanName = '/level/' + maxTrackID + '/' + device['name']
				oscAddressScopes += [deviceChanName + '/l',
									 deviceChanName + '/r']
				self.sendOSC('/tda/command', ['setDeviceNetworking',
									deviceLomExpression + '.' + expression])

			self.OSCDeviceInCHOP.par.oscaddressscope = \
													' '.join(oscAddressScopes)
			self.ChanNameCHOP.par.Channelnames = ' '.join(
						[self.ChanNameStripped(address,
								self.ownerComp.par.Stripdeviceprefixsegments)
								  			for address in oscAddressScopes])

			# other info from device
			ownerId = self.ownerComp.id
			# Parameters (only DeviceOn for rack)
			for aParNum, info in enumerate(self.devicePars[:1]):
				if info is None:
					continue
				if len(info) == 1:
					info.append(info[0])
				par = getattr(self.ownerComp.par, info[0])
				par.enable = True
				returnAddress = returnAddressBase + '/' + info[1] + '/value'
				listener = (deviceLomExpression + '.parameters['
						+ str(aParNum) + ']', 'value', returnAddress, ownerId)
				self.addListener(listener, par)

			# macro controls
			macroAPars = list(rackInfo['aPars'].keys())
			# we have to search for indexes because names can change
			macroPars = []
			for aParName in macroAPars:
				macroPar = rackInfo['aPars'][aParName]
				if 0 < macroPar['index'] < 9:
					macroPars.append(macroPar)
			for macroPar in macroPars:
				lomExpression = deviceLomExpression + '.' \
								+ macroPar['expression']
				valueAddress = returnAddressBase + '/' + \
								self.validChannelName(macroPar['name']) \
							   	+ '/value'
				# value listener
				listener = (lomExpression, 'value',
							valueAddress, ownerId)
				par = getattr(self.ownerComp.par, 'Macro' +
							  						str(macroPar['index']))
				par.label = macroPar['name']
				self.updateMacroBinds(par,
									  oldMacroLabels[macroPar['index'] - 1])
				par.enable = True
				self.addListener(listener, par, macroPar['min'],
								 macroPar['max'])

		else:
			# process device
			lomInfo = deviceInfo['lomInfo']
			lomExpression = deviceInfo['lomExpression']
			returnAddressBase = deviceInfo['returnAddress']
			self.SendDevices[lomInfo['name']] = lomInfo

			# make sure device has correct address.
			self.sendOSC('/tda/command', ['setDeviceNetworking', lomExpression])

			# device channel name
			chanName = '/level/' + maxTrackID + '/' + lomInfo['name']
			oscAddressScopes = [chanName + '/l', chanName +'/r']
			self.OSCDeviceInCHOP.par.oscaddressscope = \
													' '.join(oscAddressScopes)
			self.deviceOutAddress = chanName
			self.ChanNameCHOP.par.Channelnames = ' '.join(
						[self.ChanNameStripped(address,
								self.ownerComp.par.Stripdeviceprefixsegments)
								  			for address in oscAddressScopes])

			# other info from device
			# Parameters
			ownerId = self.ownerComp.id
			for aParNum, info in enumerate(self.devicePars):
				if info is None:
					continue
				if len(info) == 1:
					info.append(info[0])
				par = getattr(self.ownerComp.par, info[0])
				par.enable = True
				returnAddress = returnAddressBase + '/' + info[1] + '/value'
				listener = (lomExpression + '.parameters[' + str(aParNum) + ']',
							'value', returnAddress, ownerId)
				self.addListener(listener, par)

			# name
			self.ownerComp.par.Devicename.enable = True
			self.ownerComp.par.Devicename = self.getMenuLabel(
							getattr(self.ownerComp.par, self.FinalDevicePar))

		# oscin ready to go
		self.checkDeviceEnable()

	def onDeviceInvalid(self):
		"""
		Call this when we don't have a valid device, to disable stuff
		:return:
		"""
		for info in self.devicePars:
			if info is not None:
				getattr(self.ownerComp.par, info[0]).enable = False
		self.ownerComp.par.Devicename = ''
		self.ownerComp.par.Devicename.enable = False
		self.OSCDeviceInCHOP.par.oscaddressscope = ''
		self.checkDeviceEnable()
		self.deviceOutAddress = ''

	def onAbletonNotify(self, info):
		TDAbletonCompBaseExt.onAbletonNotify(self, info)
		# Set device ports when connected
		if info['notificationType'] == 'connected':
			self.checkDeviceEnable()
			self.OSCDeviceInCHOP.par.port = self.TDAbletonComp.par.Inport.eval()
		elif info['notificationType'] == 'disconnected':
			self.checkDeviceEnable()
		elif info['notificationType'] == 'lomNameChanged':
			changedObj = info
			deviceInfo = self.deviceInfo
			if not deviceInfo:
				return
			devicePtr = deviceInfo['lomInfo']['ptr']
			if not self.isRackSet and changedObj['ptr'] == devicePtr:
				self.ownerComp.par.Devicename = changedObj['name']
				validName = self.validChannelName(changedObj['name'])
				if info['name'] != validName:
					self.setObjectName(validName)
			elif self.isRackSet:
				# check for rack macro name changes
				try:
					changedObj['parentInfo']['ptr']
				except:
					return
				if changedObj['parentInfo']['ptr'] == devicePtr:
					self.setupListeners()
				else:
					# check for rack level device name changes
					try:
						changedObj['parentInfo']['parentInfo']['ptr']
					except:
						return
					if changedObj['parentInfo']['parentInfo']['ptr'] == \
																devicePtr:
						validName = self.validChannelName(changedObj['name'])
						if info['name'] != validName:
							lomExpression = deviceInfo['lomExpression'] + '.' + \
									changedObj['parentInfo']['expression'] + '.' + \
									changedObj['expression']
							self.setObjectName(validName, lomExpression)
						else:
							self.setupListeners()

	def clearOSCIn(self):
		self.OSCDeviceInCHOP.par.resetpulse.pulse()
		TDAbletonCompBaseExt.clearOSCIn(self)

	def onParPulse(self, par):
		if par.name == 'Adddevice':
			if not self.TDAbletonComp.Connected:
				return
			TDAbletonCompBaseExt.addDevice(self, self.deviceName + '.amxd',
										   'TDA_Level')
		elif par.name == 'Addrack':
			if not self.TDAbletonComp.Connected:
				return
			TDAbletonCompBaseExt.addDevice(self, self.rackName + '.adg',
										   'TDA_Audio_Analyzer')
		else:
			TDAbletonCompBaseExt.onParPulse(self, par)

	def onParValueChange(self, par, val, prev):
		if par.name == 'Devicename':
			# make sure device name is a valid channel name or there'll be
			# problems receiving data
			deviceInfo = self.deviceInfo
			if deviceInfo:
				val = self.validChannelName(val)
				if par.eval() != val or deviceInfo['lomInfo']['name'] != val:
					self.setObjectName(val)
		elif par.name == 'Output':
			self.updateLOMPars()
		elif par.name == 'Connect':
			self.checkDeviceEnable()
			TDAbletonCompBaseExt.onParValueChange(self, par, val, prev)
		elif par.name == 'Stripdeviceprefixsegments':
			self.OSCDeviceInCHOP.par.resetpulse.pulse()
			if self.ownerComp.par.Connect:
				self.setupListeners()
		else:
			TDAbletonCompBaseExt.onParValueChange(self, par, val, prev)

	def checkDeviceEnable(self):
		self.OutCHOP.cook(force=True)
		self.OSCDeviceInCHOP.cook(force=True)

	def setObjectName(self, name, lomExpression=None):
		"""
		Change device's name
		:param name: new name
		:param lomExpression: expression to object or None to use FinalDevicePar
		:return:
		"""
		if lomExpression is None:
			lomExpression = self.LOMParInfo(
										self.FinalDevicePar)['lomExpression']
		command = lomExpression + '.name = "' + name + '"'
		self.runRemoteCode(command)

	@property
	def deviceOutAddressStripped(self):
		strips = self.ownerComp.par.Stripdeviceprefixsegments.eval()
		address = self.deviceOutAddress[1:]
		for i in range(strips):
			slash = address.find('/')
			if slash == -1:
				address = ''
			else:
				address = address[slash + 1:]
		if address:
			return address + '/'
		else:
			return ''

	def updateMacroBinds(self, par, oldLabel):
		if oldLabel == par.label:
			return
		bindChain = TDF.bindChain(par, parsOnly=True)
		done = set()
		def updateBindReferences(targetPar):
			if targetPar in done:
				return
			done.add(targetPar)
			if targetPar.label == oldLabel:
				try:
					targetPar.label = par.label
				except:
					# builtin parameter or some other nonsense
					pass
			for bindRef in targetPar.bindReferences:
				if isinstance(bindRef, Par):
					updateBindReferences(bindRef)

		updateBindReferences(bindChain[-1])

*�����������������"# me - this DAT
# par - the Par object that has changed
# val - the current value
# prev - the previous value
# 
# Make sure the corresponding toggle is enabled in the Parameter Execute DAT.

def onValueChange(par, val, prev):
	ext.TDAbletonCompBaseExt.onParValueChange(par, val, prev)
	return

def onPulse(par):
	ext.TDAbletonCompBaseExt.onParPulse(par)
	return

def onExpressionChange(par, val, prev):
	return

def onExportChange(par, val, prev):
	return

*�����������������00#AbletonTrackExt

TDF = op.TDModules.mod.TDFunctions

TDAbletonCompBaseExt = \
					mod('abletonBase/TDAbletonCompBaseExt').TDAbletonCompBaseExt

CallbacksExt = mod('CallbacksExt').CallbacksExt

class AbletonTrackExt(CallbacksExt, TDAbletonCompBaseExt):
	"""
	AbletonTrackExt sets up a 2 way connection with an Ableton track.
	"""
	def __init__(self, ownerComp):
		# The component to which this extension is attached
		CallbacksExt.__init__(self, ownerComp)
		TDAbletonCompBaseExt.__init__(self, ownerComp)
		self.mergeCHOP = self.ownerComp.op('merge_addDefaults')
		self.clipStatusDAT = self.ownerComp.op('merge_statusCol')

		TDF.createProperty(self, 'ReturnAddress', value='', readOnly=True,
						   									dependable=True)
		# clip stuff
		TDF.createProperty(self, 'ClipID', value='_', readOnly=True,
						   									dependable=True)
		self.clipListeners = []
		self.ClipsTable = self.ownerComp.op('clipsTable')
		self.clipProps = ['playing_position', 'start_marker', 'end_marker',
					 		'loop_start', 'loop_end']
		self.clipListeners = [] # clip listener keys
		TDF.createProperty(self, 'ClipExpression', value='',
						readOnly=True, dependable=True)
		TDF.createProperty(self, 'PlayingSlotIndex', value=-1,
						readOnly=True, dependable=True)
		TDF.createProperty(self, 'FiredSlotIndex', value=-1,
						readOnly=True, dependable=True)

		TDF.createProperty(self, 'ClipSlots', value=[], readOnly=True,
						   									dependable=True)
		TDF.createProperty(self, 'WatchClips', value=False, readOnly=True,
						   									dependable=True)
		self._WatchClips.val = hasattr(self.ownerComp.par, 'Includeclipdata') \
							   and self.ownerComp.par.Includeclipdata.eval()
		# store these for when we need to do stuff with clips etc.
		self.lomExpression = ''
		# key for color listener (needed to set color)
		self.colorListener = None
		

	def setupListeners(self):
		# grab index before clear

		self.colorListener = None
		self.onColorChange()
		self.onClipColorChange()
		# make sure we have a valid track
		chainInfo = self.LOMParInfo(self.FinalChainPar)
		if not chainInfo:
			for parName in ['Mute', 'Solo', 'Crossfader', 'Cuevolume',
							'Panning', 'Trackactivator', 'Volume', 'Colorr']:
				getattr(self.ownerComp.par, parName).enable = False
			self._ClipSlots.val = []
			self.disconnect()
			self._ReturnAddress.val = ''
			return

		oldReturnAddress = self.ReturnAddress

		# returnAddress: osc address that updates will be sent to from tda.
		# note that this must be a valid CHOP channel name
		self._ReturnAddress.val = returnAddress = chainInfo['returnAddress']

		if self.mergeCHOP:
			# do this before channels get wiped
			clipIndexChannel = self.mergeCHOP.chan('*playing_slot_index')
			if clipIndexChannel is not None and \
											oldReturnAddress == returnAddress:
				index = int(clipIndexChannel.eval())
			else:
				index = -1

		TDAbletonCompBaseExt.setupListeners(self)

		# use this to figure out what kind of chain we're dealing with
		chainExpression = chainInfo['lomInfo']['expression']

		# set up listener parameters
		# lomExpression: a Python expression to the live object in tda
		self.lomExpression = lomExpression = chainInfo['lomExpression']

		# id: unique id of the object requesting a listener
		ownerId = self.ownerComp.id
		# read only properties
		readOnlyProps = []

		# tracks
		if self.FinalChainPar == 'Track' \
					and self.ownerComp.par.Includeaudiodata.eval() \
					and not chainInfo['lomInfo']['hasMIDIOutput']:
			readOnlyProps = ['output_meter_left', 'output_meter_right',
							 'output_meter_level']
		# audio and midi tracks
		if chainExpression.startswith('track') and \
								hasattr(self.ownerComp.par, 'Includeclipdata'):
			readOnlyProps += ['playing_slot_index', 'fired_slot_index']
			self.clipListeners = [] # was already cleared in base setupListeners
			self._setClipSlots()
			self.setClipListeners(index)
			self._WatchClips.val = self.ownerComp.par.Includeclipdata.eval()
		else:
			self._ClipSlots.val = []
			self._WatchClips.val = False

		for prop in readOnlyProps:
			listener = (lomExpression, prop, returnAddress + '/' + prop,
						ownerId)
			self.addListener(listener, None)

		self.ownerComp.par.Colorr.enable = True
		self.colorListener = (lomExpression, 'color', 
								returnAddress + '/chain_color', ownerId)
		self.addListener(self.colorListener, None)

		for property in  ['mute', 'solo']:
			par = getattr(self.ownerComp.par, property.capitalize())
			if chainExpression.startswith('master_track'):
				par.enable = False
			else:
				listener = (lomExpression, property,
									returnAddress + '/' + property, ownerId)
				par.enable = True
				self.addListener(listener, par)

		# mixer stuff
		device = chainInfo['lomInfo']['devices']['# Mixer #']
		lomExpression += '.mixer_device.'
		for mixerProp in ['Crossfader', 'Cue Volume', 'Panning',
						  						'Track Activator', 'Volume']:
			parName = mixerProp.replace(' ', '').capitalize()
			returnAddressName = mixerProp.replace(' ', '_')
			par = getattr(self.ownerComp.par, parName)
			if mixerProp not in device['aPars']:
				par.enable = False
				par.val = 0
				continue
			par.enable = True
			propInfo = device['aPars'][mixerProp]
			mixerExpression = lomExpression + propInfo['expression']
			mixerAddress = returnAddress + '/__Mixer__/' + \
						   					returnAddressName + '/value'
			self.addListener((mixerExpression, 'value', mixerAddress, ownerId),
								par, propInfo['min'], propInfo['max'])

	def onParValueChange(self, par, val, prev):
		if par.name in ['Includeaudiodata', 'Includeclipdata'] \
															and self.Connected:
			self.setupListeners()
			self.clearOSCIn()
		elif par.name.startswith('Color'):
			if self.colorListener in self.Listeners:
				i = self.ConvertRGBToInt(
						self.ownerComp.par.Colorr.eval(),
						self.ownerComp.par.Colorg.eval(),
						self.ownerComp.par.Colorb.eval()
				)
				self.setListener(self.colorListener, i, ignoreErrors=True)
				#self.onColorChange()
		elif par.name.startswith('Clipcolor'):
			if par.enable:
				i = self.ConvertRGBToInt(
						self.ownerComp.par.Clipcolorr.eval(),
						self.ownerComp.par.Clipcolorg.eval(),
						self.ownerComp.par.Clipcolorb.eval()
				)
				self.runRemoteCode(self.ClipExpression + '.color = ' + str(i))
		else:
			TDAbletonCompBaseExt.onParValueChange(self, par, val, prev)

	def onParPulse(self, par):
		if par.name == 'Stopallclips':
			self.StopAllClips()
		else:
			TDAbletonCompBaseExt.onParPulse(self, par)

	def onOutputsChannelInit(self, channel, value):	
		if channel.endswith('chain_color'):
			self.onColorChange()
		elif channel.endswith('/color'):
			self.onClipColorChange()
		else:
			TDAbletonCompBaseExt.onOutputsChannelInit(self, channel, value)	

	def onColorChange(self, chanName='*chain_color', parName='Color'):
		try:
			val = self.OutCHOP.chans(chanName)[0].eval()
		except:
			val = 0
			self.ownerComp.par[f'{parName}r'].enable = False
		else:
			if val == -1:
				val = 0
				self.ownerComp.par[f'{parName}r'].enable = False
		self.ownerComp.par[f'{parName}r'], self.ownerComp.par[f'{parName}g'], \
				self.ownerComp.par[f'{parName}b'] = \
												self.ConvertIntToRGB(int(val))

	def onClipColorChange(self):
		if self.ownerComp.par['Clipcolorr'] is None:
			return
		self.onColorChange('*/color', 'Clipcolor')

	def onAbletonNotify(self, info):
		TDAbletonCompBaseExt.onAbletonNotify(self, info)
		# Set midi ports when connected
		if info['notificationType'] in ['clipSlotChanged']:
			try:
				trackPtr = int(self.ownerComp.par.Track.eval())
			except:
				pass
			else:
				if info['parentInfo']['ptr'] == trackPtr:
					self._clipSlotChanged(info)
		# elif info['notificationType'] == 'connected':
		# 	self.setClipListeners()

	def _clipSlotChanged(self, info):
		self._ClipSlots.modified()

	def _setClipSlots(self):
		self._ClipSlots.val = self.LOMParInfo('Track')['lomInfo']['clipSlots']

	def setClipListeners(self, index=None):
		if not self.Connected:
			self.onClipColorChange()
			return
		# remove old listeners
		for listener in self.clipListeners:
			if listener in self.Listeners:
				self.removeListener(listener)
		# figure out clip expression
		if index is None:
			index = self.PlayingSlotIndex
		if self.ClipID != str(index):
			for name in [c.name for c in self.OSCInCHOP.chans('*__clip__*')]:
				self.OSCInCHOP[name].destroy()	
			self._ClipID.val = str(index) if index >= 0 else '_'
		if index >= 0 and self.ClipSlots[index]['clip']:
			clipExpression = self.lomExpression + '.clip_slots[' + \
							 							str(index) + '].clip'
		else:
			clipExpression = ''
		self._ClipExpression.val = clipExpression
		if clipExpression and index >= 0:
			self.ownerComp.par.Clipcolorr.enable = True		
			if self.ownerComp.par.Includeclipdata.eval():
				self.clipListeners = []
				for prop in self.clipProps:
					par = getattr(self.ownerComp.par,
								prop.replace('_', '').capitalize(), None)
					listener = (clipExpression, prop,
							self.ReturnAddress + f'/__clip__/{index}/' + prop,
							self.ownerComp.id)
					self.clipListeners.append(listener)
					if par is not None:
						self.addListener(listener, par)
						par.enable = True
					else:
						self.addListener(listener)
		else:
			try:
				self.OSCInCHOP.chans('*/color')[0].destroy()
			except:
				pass
			self.ownerComp.par.Clipcolorr.enable = False		
			for prop in self.clipProps:
				par = getattr(self.ownerComp.par,
							prop.replace('_', '').capitalize(), None)
				if par is not None:
					par.enable = False
			self.clipListeners = []
		self.onClipColorChange()

	def onClipStatusChange(self):
		try:
			playingSlot = int(self.mergeCHOP.chan('*playing_slot_index'))

		except:
			playingSlot = -1
		try:
			firedSlot = int(self.mergeCHOP.chan('*fired_slot_index'))
		except:
			firedSlot = -1

		# callbacks
		if playingSlot != self.PlayingSlotIndex:
			doCallback = self.ownerComp.par.Enablecallbacks and \
						 not(playingSlot < 0 and self.PlayingSlotIndex < 0)
			self._PlayingSlotIndex.val = playingSlot
			self.setClipListeners()
			if doCallback:
				try:
					clipInfo = self.ClipSlots[playingSlot]['clip'] \
													if playingSlot >=0 else None
				except:
					clipInfo = None
				if clipInfo:
					infoDict = {'name': clipInfo['name'],
								'filePath': clipInfo['filePath'],
								'color': clipInfo['color'],
								'index': playingSlot}
				else:
					infoDict = {'name': None,
								'filePath': None,
								'color': None,
								'index': playingSlot}
				self.DoCallback('onPlayingClipChanged', infoDict)
		if firedSlot != self.FiredSlotIndex:
			doCallback = self.ownerComp.par.Enablecallbacks and \
						 not(firedSlot < 0 and self.FiredSlotIndex < 0)
			self._FiredSlotIndex.val = firedSlot
			if doCallback:
				try:
					clipInfo = self.ClipSlots[firedSlot]['clip'] \
													if firedSlot >=0 else None
				except:
					clipInfo = None
				if clipInfo:
					infoDict = {'name': clipInfo['name'],
								'filePath': clipInfo['filePath'],
								'color': clipInfo['color'],
								'index': firedSlot}
				else:
					infoDict = {'name': None,
								'filePath': None,
								'color': None,
								'index': firedSlot}
				self.DoCallback('onFiredClipChanged', infoDict)


	def FireClipSlot(self, clipSlot):
		"""
		Fire (trigger) the clip slot at index clipSlot
		"""
		if clipSlot < len(self.ClipSlots) and self.lomExpression:
			self.runRemoteCode(self.lomExpression +
							   '.clip_slots[' + str(clipSlot) + '].fire()')
		else:
			debug("FireClipSlot - Invalid clip slot:", clipSlot)

	def StopClipSlot(self, clipSlot):
		"""
		Stop the clip slot at index clipSlot
		"""
		if clipSlot < len(self.ClipSlots) and self.lomExpression:
			self.runRemoteCode(self.lomExpression +
							   '.clip_slots[' + str(clipSlot) + '].stop()')
		else:
			debug("FireClipSlot - Invalid clip slot:", clipSlot)

	def StopAllClips(self):
		"""
		Stop all clips from playing
		"""
		if self.lomExpression:
			self.runRemoteCode(self.lomExpression + '.stop_all_clips()')

	@property
	def ClipPrefix(self):
		try:
			return self.ChanNameStripped(self.ReturnAddress + '/__clip__/') + \
					self.ClipID + '/'
		except:
			return ''

	@property
	def ClipColor(self):
		cell = self.ClipsTable[self.ClipID, 4]
		if cell is None or cell.val == '':
			return -1
		else:
			return cell.val
