2
*�����������������00#AbletonTrackExt

TDF = op.TDModules.mod.TDFunctions

TDAbletonCompBaseExt = \
					mod('abletonBase/TDAbletonCompBaseExt').TDAbletonCompBaseExt

CallbacksExt = mod('CallbacksExt').CallbacksExt

class AbletonTrackExt(CallbacksExt, TDAbletonCompBaseExt):
	"""
	AbletonTrackExt sets up a 2 way connection with an Ableton track.
	"""
	def __init__(self, ownerComp):
		# The component to which this extension is attached
		CallbacksExt.__init__(self, ownerComp)
		TDAbletonCompBaseExt.__init__(self, ownerComp)
		self.mergeCHOP = self.ownerComp.op('merge_addDefaults')
		self.clipStatusDAT = self.ownerComp.op('merge_statusCol')

		TDF.createProperty(self, 'ReturnAddress', value='', readOnly=True,
						   									dependable=True)
		# clip stuff
		TDF.createProperty(self, 'ClipID', value='_', readOnly=True,
						   									dependable=True)
		self.clipListeners = []
		self.ClipsTable = self.ownerComp.op('clipsTable')
		self.clipProps = ['playing_position', 'start_marker', 'end_marker',
					 		'loop_start', 'loop_end']
		self.clipListeners = [] # clip listener keys
		TDF.createProperty(self, 'ClipExpression', value='',
						readOnly=True, dependable=True)
		TDF.createProperty(self, 'PlayingSlotIndex', value=-1,
						readOnly=True, dependable=True)
		TDF.createProperty(self, 'FiredSlotIndex', value=-1,
						readOnly=True, dependable=True)

		TDF.createProperty(self, 'ClipSlots', value=[], readOnly=True,
						   									dependable=True)
		TDF.createProperty(self, 'WatchClips', value=False, readOnly=True,
						   									dependable=True)
		self._WatchClips.val = hasattr(self.ownerComp.par, 'Includeclipdata') \
							   and self.ownerComp.par.Includeclipdata.eval()
		# store these for when we need to do stuff with clips etc.
		self.lomExpression = ''
		# key for color listener (needed to set color)
		self.colorListener = None
		

	def setupListeners(self):
		# grab index before clear

		self.colorListener = None
		self.onColorChange()
		self.onClipColorChange()
		# make sure we have a valid track
		chainInfo = self.LOMParInfo(self.FinalChainPar)
		if not chainInfo:
			for parName in ['Mute', 'Solo', 'Crossfader', 'Cuevolume',
							'Panning', 'Trackactivator', 'Volume', 'Colorr']:
				getattr(self.ownerComp.par, parName).enable = False
			self._ClipSlots.val = []
			self.disconnect()
			self._ReturnAddress.val = ''
			return

		oldReturnAddress = self.ReturnAddress

		# returnAddress: osc address that updates will be sent to from tda.
		# note that this must be a valid CHOP channel name
		self._ReturnAddress.val = returnAddress = chainInfo['returnAddress']

		if self.mergeCHOP:
			# do this before channels get wiped
			clipIndexChannel = self.mergeCHOP.chan('*playing_slot_index')
			if clipIndexChannel is not None and \
											oldReturnAddress == returnAddress:
				index = int(clipIndexChannel.eval())
			else:
				index = -1

		TDAbletonCompBaseExt.setupListeners(self)

		# use this to figure out what kind of chain we're dealing with
		chainExpression = chainInfo['lomInfo']['expression']

		# set up listener parameters
		# lomExpression: a Python expression to the live object in tda
		self.lomExpression = lomExpression = chainInfo['lomExpression']

		# id: unique id of the object requesting a listener
		ownerId = self.ownerComp.id
		# read only properties
		readOnlyProps = []

		# tracks
		if self.FinalChainPar == 'Track' \
					and self.ownerComp.par.Includeaudiodata.eval() \
					and not chainInfo['lomInfo']['hasMIDIOutput']:
			readOnlyProps = ['output_meter_left', 'output_meter_right',
							 'output_meter_level']
		# audio and midi tracks
		if chainExpression.startswith('track') and \
								hasattr(self.ownerComp.par, 'Includeclipdata'):
			readOnlyProps += ['playing_slot_index', 'fired_slot_index']
			self.clipListeners = [] # was already cleared in base setupListeners
			self._setClipSlots()
			self.setClipListeners(index)
			self._WatchClips.val = self.ownerComp.par.Includeclipdata.eval()
		else:
			self._ClipSlots.val = []
			self._WatchClips.val = False

		for prop in readOnlyProps:
			listener = (lomExpression, prop, returnAddress + '/' + prop,
						ownerId)
			self.addListener(listener, None)

		self.ownerComp.par.Colorr.enable = True
		self.colorListener = (lomExpression, 'color', 
								returnAddress + '/chain_color', ownerId)
		self.addListener(self.colorListener, None)

		for property in  ['mute', 'solo']:
			par = getattr(self.ownerComp.par, property.capitalize())
			if chainExpression.startswith('master_track'):
				par.enable = False
			else:
				listener = (lomExpression, property,
									returnAddress + '/' + property, ownerId)
				par.enable = True
				self.addListener(listener, par)

		# mixer stuff
		device = chainInfo['lomInfo']['devices']['# Mixer #']
		lomExpression += '.mixer_device.'
		for mixerProp in ['Crossfader', 'Cue Volume', 'Panning',
						  						'Track Activator', 'Volume']:
			parName = mixerProp.replace(' ', '').capitalize()
			returnAddressName = mixerProp.replace(' ', '_')
			par = getattr(self.ownerComp.par, parName)
			if mixerProp not in device['aPars']:
				par.enable = False
				par.val = 0
				continue
			par.enable = True
			propInfo = device['aPars'][mixerProp]
			mixerExpression = lomExpression + propInfo['expression']
			mixerAddress = returnAddress + '/__Mixer__/' + \
						   					returnAddressName + '/value'
			self.addListener((mixerExpression, 'value', mixerAddress, ownerId),
								par, propInfo['min'], propInfo['max'])

	def onParValueChange(self, par, val, prev):
		if par.name in ['Includeaudiodata', 'Includeclipdata'] \
															and self.Connected:
			self.setupListeners()
			self.clearOSCIn()
		elif par.name.startswith('Color'):
			if self.colorListener in self.Listeners:
				i = self.ConvertRGBToInt(
						self.ownerComp.par.Colorr.eval(),
						self.ownerComp.par.Colorg.eval(),
						self.ownerComp.par.Colorb.eval()
				)
				self.setListener(self.colorListener, i, ignoreErrors=True)
				#self.onColorChange()
		elif par.name.startswith('Clipcolor'):
			if par.enable:
				i = self.ConvertRGBToInt(
						self.ownerComp.par.Clipcolorr.eval(),
						self.ownerComp.par.Clipcolorg.eval(),
						self.ownerComp.par.Clipcolorb.eval()
				)
				self.runRemoteCode(self.ClipExpression + '.color = ' + str(i))
		else:
			TDAbletonCompBaseExt.onParValueChange(self, par, val, prev)

	def onParPulse(self, par):
		if par.name == 'Stopallclips':
			self.StopAllClips()
		else:
			TDAbletonCompBaseExt.onParPulse(self, par)

	def onOutputsChannelInit(self, channel, value):	
		if channel.endswith('chain_color'):
			self.onColorChange()
		elif channel.endswith('/color'):
			self.onClipColorChange()
		else:
			TDAbletonCompBaseExt.onOutputsChannelInit(self, channel, value)	

	def onColorChange(self, chanName='*chain_color', parName='Color'):
		try:
			val = self.OutCHOP.chans(chanName)[0].eval()
		except:
			val = 0
			self.ownerComp.par[f'{parName}r'].enable = False
		else:
			if val == -1:
				val = 0
				self.ownerComp.par[f'{parName}r'].enable = False
		self.ownerComp.par[f'{parName}r'], self.ownerComp.par[f'{parName}g'], \
				self.ownerComp.par[f'{parName}b'] = \
												self.ConvertIntToRGB(int(val))

	def onClipColorChange(self):
		if self.ownerComp.par['Clipcolorr'] is None:
			return
		self.onColorChange('*/color', 'Clipcolor')

	def onAbletonNotify(self, info):
		TDAbletonCompBaseExt.onAbletonNotify(self, info)
		# Set midi ports when connected
		if info['notificationType'] in ['clipSlotChanged']:
			try:
				trackPtr = int(self.ownerComp.par.Track.eval())
			except:
				pass
			else:
				if info['parentInfo']['ptr'] == trackPtr:
					self._clipSlotChanged(info)
		# elif info['notificationType'] == 'connected':
		# 	self.setClipListeners()

	def _clipSlotChanged(self, info):
		self._ClipSlots.modified()

	def _setClipSlots(self):
		self._ClipSlots.val = self.LOMParInfo('Track')['lomInfo']['clipSlots']

	def setClipListeners(self, index=None):
		if not self.Connected:
			self.onClipColorChange()
			return
		# remove old listeners
		for listener in self.clipListeners:
			if listener in self.Listeners:
				self.removeListener(listener)
		# figure out clip expression
		if index is None:
			index = self.PlayingSlotIndex
		if self.ClipID != str(index):
			for name in [c.name for c in self.OSCInCHOP.chans('*__clip__*')]:
				self.OSCInCHOP[name].destroy()	
			self._ClipID.val = str(index) if index >= 0 else '_'
		if index >= 0 and self.ClipSlots[index]['clip']:
			clipExpression = self.lomExpression + '.clip_slots[' + \
							 							str(index) + '].clip'
		else:
			clipExpression = ''
		self._ClipExpression.val = clipExpression
		if clipExpression and index >= 0:
			self.ownerComp.par.Clipcolorr.enable = True		
			if self.ownerComp.par.Includeclipdata.eval():
				self.clipListeners = []
				for prop in self.clipProps:
					par = getattr(self.ownerComp.par,
								prop.replace('_', '').capitalize(), None)
					listener = (clipExpression, prop,
							self.ReturnAddress + f'/__clip__/{index}/' + prop,
							self.ownerComp.id)
					self.clipListeners.append(listener)
					if par is not None:
						self.addListener(listener, par)
						par.enable = True
					else:
						self.addListener(listener)
		else:
			try:
				self.OSCInCHOP.chans('*/color')[0].destroy()
			except:
				pass
			self.ownerComp.par.Clipcolorr.enable = False		
			for prop in self.clipProps:
				par = getattr(self.ownerComp.par,
							prop.replace('_', '').capitalize(), None)
				if par is not None:
					par.enable = False
			self.clipListeners = []
		self.onClipColorChange()

	def onClipStatusChange(self):
		try:
			playingSlot = int(self.mergeCHOP.chan('*playing_slot_index'))

		except:
			playingSlot = -1
		try:
			firedSlot = int(self.mergeCHOP.chan('*fired_slot_index'))
		except:
			firedSlot = -1

		# callbacks
		if playingSlot != self.PlayingSlotIndex:
			doCallback = self.ownerComp.par.Enablecallbacks and \
						 not(playingSlot < 0 and self.PlayingSlotIndex < 0)
			self._PlayingSlotIndex.val = playingSlot
			self.setClipListeners()
			if doCallback:
				try:
					clipInfo = self.ClipSlots[playingSlot]['clip'] \
													if playingSlot >=0 else None
				except:
					clipInfo = None
				if clipInfo:
					infoDict = {'name': clipInfo['name'],
								'filePath': clipInfo['filePath'],
								'color': clipInfo['color'],
								'index': playingSlot}
				else:
					infoDict = {'name': None,
								'filePath': None,
								'color': None,
								'index': playingSlot}
				self.DoCallback('onPlayingClipChanged', infoDict)
		if firedSlot != self.FiredSlotIndex:
			doCallback = self.ownerComp.par.Enablecallbacks and \
						 not(firedSlot < 0 and self.FiredSlotIndex < 0)
			self._FiredSlotIndex.val = firedSlot
			if doCallback:
				try:
					clipInfo = self.ClipSlots[firedSlot]['clip'] \
													if firedSlot >=0 else None
				except:
					clipInfo = None
				if clipInfo:
					infoDict = {'name': clipInfo['name'],
								'filePath': clipInfo['filePath'],
								'color': clipInfo['color'],
								'index': firedSlot}
				else:
					infoDict = {'name': None,
								'filePath': None,
								'color': None,
								'index': firedSlot}
				self.DoCallback('onFiredClipChanged', infoDict)


	def FireClipSlot(self, clipSlot):
		"""
		Fire (trigger) the clip slot at index clipSlot
		"""
		if clipSlot < len(self.ClipSlots) and self.lomExpression:
			self.runRemoteCode(self.lomExpression +
							   '.clip_slots[' + str(clipSlot) + '].fire()')
		else:
			debug("FireClipSlot - Invalid clip slot:", clipSlot)

	def StopClipSlot(self, clipSlot):
		"""
		Stop the clip slot at index clipSlot
		"""
		if clipSlot < len(self.ClipSlots) and self.lomExpression:
			self.runRemoteCode(self.lomExpression +
							   '.clip_slots[' + str(clipSlot) + '].stop()')
		else:
			debug("FireClipSlot - Invalid clip slot:", clipSlot)

	def StopAllClips(self):
		"""
		Stop all clips from playing
		"""
		if self.lomExpression:
			self.runRemoteCode(self.lomExpression + '.stop_all_clips()')

	@property
	def ClipPrefix(self):
		try:
			return self.ChanNameStripped(self.ReturnAddress + '/__clip__/') + \
					self.ClipID + '/'
		except:
			return ''

	@property
	def ClipColor(self):
		cell = self.ClipsTable[self.ClipID, 4]
		if cell is None or cell.val == '':
			return -1
		else:
			return cell.val

2
*�����������������+1.27
allow "Custom" Live Install

1.28
scrollable console
abletonMIDI split touching notes
numerator/denominator 
(ableton values updated when auto-sync re-enabled)
ignoreErrors on setListener
Color and Clip Color on track, chain, clipslot
backwards compatibility: clip # in osc
Disconnect before update all

1.30
-Python 3 compatibility
-update clip notes system for Live 11
-discontinue abletonLog comp
-fix default range par positions on abletonMIDI
-Print device dict on not found. also use isLoadable and remove .amxd when looking for devices in TDA.py
-Move console and ping to utilities page
-Remove IP display in TDA_Master (Ableton ruined that for us)
-fixed hasattr checks in TDA

1.30b
- fix problems with midi note splitting
- side-by-side install for beta versions

1.31b
- deal with beta folders in preferences

1.32
- MIDI note/cc ranger pulses
- exporter for toxes

1.33
- Node Error On Disconnect par to suppress error flags
- Automatic load Live session options on Utilities page
- Better handling of multiple plugins with same name on a single track/chain. GOTCHA: if you rearrange their order, TDAbleton may get confused.
- Python uses dict instead of OrderedDict everywhere
- BACKWARDS COMPATIBILITY WARNING in abletonSong cuepoint callbacks: cuePointNum now holds index into cuepoint table DAT output.

*����������������� # Text Component callbacks

def onValueChange(comp, value, prevValue):
	"""
	Called whenever the text component's value parameter changes for any reason including typing
	in the text window or changing the parameter directly. The callback is equivalent to the
	onValueChange in the parameter exectute DAT.
	
	Args:
		comp: the text component
		value: the new value
		prevValue: the previous value
	"""
	#debug('\nonValueChange comp:', comp.path, '- new value: ', value, ', prev value: ', prevValue )
	return
	
def onFocus(comp):
	"""
	Called when one of the text components viewers gets keyboard focus. 
	
	Args:
		comp: the text component
	"""
	#debug('\nonFocus comp:', comp.path)
	return

def onFocusEnd(comp, info):
	"""
	Called when one of the text component's viewers loses keyboard focus. In regular editing mode
	this is right after the contents are saved back to the value parameter. This function is called
	everytime the viewer loses focus, even if the text does not change.
	
	Args:
		comp: the text component
		info: a dictionary containing information about the event including:
			reason: a string indicating what caused the viewer to lose focus e.g. 'enter', 'escape', 'tab', 'unknown'
	
	"""
	if info['reason'] == 'enter':
		parent.Dialog.OnKeyPressed('enter')
	elif info['reason'] == 'escape':
		parent.Dialog.OnKeyPressed('esc')
	return
	
def onTextEdit(comp):
	"""
	Called each time the contents of the text component viewer change i.e. when typing a character,
	deleting text, cutting or pasting. 
	
	Args:
		comp: the text component
	"""
	#debug('\nonTextEdit comp:', comp.path, '- text: ', comp.editText)
	return
	
def onTextEditEnd(comp, value, prevValue):
	"""
	Called when the user finishes editing and the value has been changed. Not called in 'Continuous Edit' mode. Use
	onTextEdit and onFocusEnd when using continuous editing.
	
	Args:
		comp: the text component
		value: the new value
		prevValue: the previous value
	"""
	#debug('\nonTextEditEnd comp:', comp.path, '- new value: ', value, ', prev value: ', prevValue )
	return

*������������������l"""
Generic docs help button and pulse par system
"""

DocsHelper = op.TDResources.mod.DocsHelper.DocsHelper

2
*�����������������w\"""
Base class for TDAbleton Components.
"""

from distutils.version import LooseVersion
import os.path

TDF = op.TDModules.mod.TDFunctions
TDJ = op.TDModules.mod.TDJSON

LOMPARS = ['Track', 'Device', 'Chain1', 'Chain1device',
		   'Chain2', 'Chain2device', 'Chain3', 'Chain3device',
		   'Chain4', 'Chain4device', 'Parameter']

class TDAbletonCompBaseExt:
	"""
	TDAbletonBaseExt provides base functionality and utilities for working with
	the TDAbleton system.
	"""

	# region Main

	def __init__(self, ownerComp):
		self.ownerComp = ownerComp
		self.lomParNames = LOMPARS
		self._deleting = False

		# {(lomExpression, property, oscAddress, id):
	 	#	  {'setterID':setterIndex, 'outPar':outPar}...}
		self.Listeners = {}
		# {channelName: parName,...}
		self.AutoSyncChannels = {}
		# {parName: [par, channelName, setterIndex, lastAbletonVal or runObj],
		# 			...}
		self.OutPars = {}

		# used for parExec_outPars list of watched pars
		TDF.createProperty(self, 'OutParNames', value=set(), dependable='deep')
		# True if Comp is connected to Ableton
		TDF.createProperty(self, 'Connected', value=False, dependable=True,
						   									readOnly=True)
		# Info about currently selected LOM objects
		TDF.createProperty(self, 'FinalChainPar', value=None, readOnly=True,
						  										dependable=True)
		TDF.createProperty(self, 'FinalDevicePar', value=None, readOnly=True,
						   										dependable=True)
		TDF.createProperty(self, 'ListenersConfirmed', value=False,
						   					readOnly=False, dependable=True)
		# operators
		self.OSCInCHOP = ownerComp.op('abletonBase/oscin_outputs')
		self.OSCInDAT = ownerComp.op('abletonBase/oscin_info')
		self.OutCHOP = ownerComp.op('out1')
		# setup
		self.setTDAbletonComp()
		self.clearOSCScope()
		# utility functions
		self.getMenuLabel = self.TDAbletonComp.mod.Utils.getMenuLabel
		self.setMenuLabel = self.TDAbletonComp.mod.Utils.setMenuLabel
		self.validChannelName = self.TDAbletonComp.mod.Utils.validChannelName
		self.sendOSC = self.TDAbletonComp.SendOSC
		run("op(" + str(self.ownerComp.id) +
						").ext.TDAbletonCompBaseExt.tryConnection()" \
						"if op(" + str(self.ownerComp.id) + ") else None",
				delayFrames=1, delayRef=op.TDResources)

	def __del__(self):
		self._deleting = True
		self.disconnect()

	def tryConnection(self):
		self.SongInfo = self.TDAbletonComp.SongInfo
		if self.ownerComp.par.Connect.eval():
			if self.TDAbletonComp.Connected:
				self.onAbletonNotify({'notificationType':'connected'})
			else:
				self.onAbletonNotify({'notificationType':'disconnected'})

	def setTDAbletonComp(self):
		try:
			self.TDAbletonComp = self.ownerComp.par.Tdabletoncomp.eval()
		except:
			self.TDAbletonComp = None
		if self.TDAbletonComp is None:
			errorMsg = \
		'\n###############################################################\n'\
		'\tThis component requires a tdAbletonPackage in your project.\n'\
		'\t(Press Help parameter on About Page for documentation)\n'\
		'###############################################################'
			self.SetError(errorMsg)
			raise Exception(errorMsg)
		# check version and set up update par
		try:
			if not self.ownerComp.par.Version.eval():
				self.ownerComp.par.Version = '0'
		except:
			pass
		if self.ownerComp.par.clone.eval():
			masterVersion = self.ownerComp.par.clone.eval().par.\
														Version.eval()
			self.ownerComp.par.Update.label = "Update To " + masterVersion
			self.ownerComp.par.Update.enable = True

	def runRemoteCode(self, code):
		self.TDAbletonComp.RunRemoteCode(code)

	def RunRemoteCode(self, code):
		self.runRemoteCode(code)

	def requestRemoteData(self, code, callback, asRepr=False):
		"""
		Request data from Ableton Python Remote Script shell
		:param code: an evaluatable piece of Python code
		:param callback: the callback to call with return data as argument
		:param asRepr: return data will be formatted by repr()
		"""
		self.TDAbletonComp.RequestRemoteData(code, callback, asRepr)

	def addDevice(self, fileName, deviceName='', aParDict=''):
		"""
		Add a Max device from the TouchDesigner Remote Script folder

		:param fileName: the device file name
		:param deviceName: the device's new name ('' means no change)
		:param aParDict: changes to device parameters {<aPar name>: <value>...}
		"""
		lomParInfo = self.LOMParInfo(self.FinalChainPar)
		if not deviceName:
			deviceName = os.path.splitext(os.path.basename(fileName))[0]
		safeName = deviceName
		appendNum = 2
		while safeName in lomParInfo['lomInfo']['devices']:
			safeName = deviceName + '-' + str(appendNum)
			appendNum += 1
		self.TDAbletonComp.AddTDAMaxDevice(
			lomParInfo['lomExpression'],
			fileName,
			safeName,
			aParDict)
		# set up parameter to wait for device to be found
		devicePar = getattr(self.ownerComp.par, self.FinalDevicePar)
		if safeName + ' (Not Found)' not in devicePar.menuLabels:
			devicePar.menuLabels = [safeName + ' (Not Found)'] + \
								   devicePar.menuLabels
			devicePar.menuNames = ['Not_Found'] + devicePar.menuNames
			self.updateLOMParBinds(devicePar)

		devicePar.menuIndex = 0

	def SetError(self, error='', label='TDAbletonCompBase'):
		if error == '':
			self.ownerComp.clearScriptErrors(error=label + ':*')
		else:
			# print(self.ownerComp.path + ":", error)
			self.ownerComp.addScriptError(label + ": " + error)

	def Update(self):
		self.TDAbletonComp.UpdateAbletonComps([self.ownerComp])

	def Sync(self, parName=None):
		"""
		Set outPar value to listener's chop channel.

		if listener is None (default), Sync all Listeners.
		"""
		if parName is not None:
			for listener, listenerInfo in self.Listeners.items():
				# check listeners for par
				if listenerInfo['outPar'].name == parName:
					listeners = [(listener, listenerInfo)]
		else:
			listeners = self.Listeners.items()
		for listener, listenerInfo in listeners:
			if listenerInfo['outPar'] is not None:
				if (listenerInfo['outPar'].mode in
									(ParMode.CONSTANT, ParMode.BIND)) and \
							self.OSCInCHOP.chan(listener[2][1:]) is not None:
					listenerInfo['outPar'].val = \
									self.OSCInCHOP.chan(listener[2][1:]).eval()

	def ChanNameStripped(self, name, numSegments=None):
		"""
		Strip initial segments in same way that "Strip CHOP Prefix Segments"
			parameter does. REMOVES INITIAL SLASH

		:param name: channel name you want to strip
		:param numSegments: number of segments to strip. Defaults to value of
				Strip CHOP Prefix Segments parameter
		:return: stripped channel name
		"""
		if numSegments is None:
			numSegments = self.ownerComp.par.Stripchopprefixsegments
		return '/'.join(name.split('/')[numSegments + 1:])

	# endregion

	# region Listeners and Connectors

	def addListener(self, listener, outPar=None, parMin=None, parMax=None,
													useBaseOSC=True):
		"""
		Add a listener.

		:param listener: (lomExpression, property, returnAddress, id,
							extra=None).
							Extra is space separated special instructions:
								'noinit' = prevent initial value send
								'str_for_value' = add str listener for
									parameters (returnAddress + '/str')
		:param outPar: outgoing parameter for setting listener object
		:param parMin: outPar minimum value
		:param parMax: outPar maximum value
		:return:
		"""
		# debug('add', listener)
		# print(project.pythonStack())
		self._Connected.val = True
		self.Listeners[listener] = {'setterIndex':None if useBaseOSC else -1,
									'strippedChannel':
											self.strippedChannel(listener[2])}
		self.setOutPar(listener, outPar, parMin, parMax)

		# set up OSC ins
#		self.OSCInCHOP.par.active = True
#		self.OSCInDAT.par.active = True
		if useBaseOSC:
			self._addListenerToOSCScope(listener)
			self.ListenersConfirmed = False
		# self.sendOSC('/listener/add', listener)
		run("args[0].sendOSC('/listener/add', args[1])", self, listener,
			delayFrames=2)

	def clearOSCScope(self):
		self.OSCInCHOP.par.oscaddressscope.val = ''
		self.OSCInDAT.par.addscope.val = ''

	def _addListenerToOSCScope(self, listener, add=True):
		if self._deleting:
			return
		forChop = listener[2]
		forDat = '/listener' + listener[2] + '/*'
		chopScope = self.OSCInCHOP.par.oscaddressscope.val
		datScope = self.OSCInDAT.par.addscope.val
		if add:
			if not forChop in chopScope:
				self.OSCInCHOP.par.oscaddressscope.val = \
													chopScope + ' ' + forChop
			if not forDat in datScope:
				self.OSCInDAT.par.addscope.val = \
													datScope + ' ' + forDat
		else:
			self.OSCInCHOP.par.oscaddressscope.val = \
						' '.join([s for s in chopScope.split() if s != forChop])
			self.OSCInDAT.par.addscope.val = \
						' '.join([s for s in datScope.split() if s != forDat])

	def _removeListenerFromOSCScope(self, listener):
		self._addListenerToOSCScope(listener, False)

	def refreshOSCScopes(self):
		self.clearOSCScope()
		for listener in self.Listeners.keys():
			self._addListenerToOSCScope(listener)

	def setOutPar(self, listener, outPar=None, parMin=None, parMax=None):
		"""
		Set a listener's outPar settings.

		:param listener: (lomExpression, property, returnAddress, id)
		:param outPar: outgoing parameter for setting listener object
		:param parMin: outPar minimum value
		:param parMax: outPar maximum value
		:return:
		"""
		listenerInfo = self.Listeners[listener]
		listenerInfo['outPar'] = outPar
		channel = self.strippedChannel(listener[2])
		if outPar is not None:
			self.AutoSyncChannels[channel] = outPar.name

			# set min/max of our send parameter
			if parMin is not None:
				outPar.normMin = outPar.min = parMin
				outPar.clampMin = True
			# else:
			# 	outPar.normMin = outPar.min = 0
			# 	outPar.clampMin = False
			if parMax is not None:
				outPar.normMax = outPar.max = parMax
				outPar.clampMax = True
			# else:
			# 	outPar.normMax = outPar.max = 127
			# 	outPar.clampMax = False
		else:
			if channel in self.AutoSyncChannels:
				del self.AutoSyncChannels[listener[2][1:]]

	def removeListener(self, listener):
		if self.TDAbletonComp.Connected:
			self.sendOSC('/listener/remove', listener)

		if listener in self.Listeners:
			if self.Listeners[listener]['outPar'] is not None:
				try:
					outParName = self.Listeners[listener]['outPar'].name
				except:
					return # invalid par. Comp being deleted
				else:
					if outParName in self.OutPars:
						try:
							# in case we're waiting to restore
							self.OutPars[outParName][2].kill()
						except:
							pass
						del self.OutPars[outParName]
						self.OutParNames.discard(outParName)
			if self.Listeners[listener]['strippedChannel'] \
													in self.AutoSyncChannels:
				del self.AutoSyncChannels[
								self.Listeners[listener]['strippedChannel']]
			del self.Listeners[listener]
		self._removeListenerFromOSCScope(listener)
		if not self.Listeners:
			self.disconnect()

	def disconnect(self):
		self._Connected.val = False
		# if self.OSCInCHOP:
		# 	self.OSCInCHOP.par.active = False
			# self.OSCInCHOP.par.resetpulse.pulse()
		# if self.OSCInDAT:
		# 	self.OSCInDAT.par.active = False
			# self.OSCInDAT.par.clear.pulse()
		self.OutPars.clear()
		self.OutParNames.clear()
		self.removeAllListeners()
		self.ListenersConfirmed = True

	def removeAllListeners(self):
		for listener in list(self.Listeners.keys()):
			self.removeListener(listener)

	def Reconnect(self):
		self.startConnection()

	def setupListeners(self):
		"""
		Set up all listeners used by this Component. Will be called
		automatically at appropriate times by the TDAbleton system. You will
		generally want to call the base version at the beginning of your
		overriding method.
		"""
		self.clearOSCIn()
		self.disconnect()

	def strippedChannel(self, channelName):
		# return channel after adjusted for stripped segments
		return '/'.join(channelName.split('/')
							[1 + self.ownerComp.par.Stripchopprefixsegments:])

	def MaxTrackID(self, trackInfo=None):
		"""
		Get the OSC max track ID (used before name) for the given trackInfo.
		Track IDs look like: t#-regular track, tr#-return track, tm-master

		:param trackInfo: SongInfo track dictionary. None uses this device's
							Track parameter
		:return: track ID or None if no info
		"""
		if trackInfo is None:
			trackInfo = self.LOMParInfo('Track')['lomInfo']
		if not trackInfo:
			return None
		if trackInfo['expression'].startswith('tracks'):
			return 't' + str(trackInfo['index'])
		elif trackInfo['expression'].startswith('return'):
			return 'tr' + str(trackInfo['index'])
		else:
			return 'tm'

	# endregion

	# region Live Object Model parameters

	def updateLOMPars(self, level='Song', setupListeners=True):
		"""
		Update Live Object Model parameter menus. This is called whenever LOM
		changes or a LOMPar value changes.

		level can be 'Song', 'Track', 'Device', or 'Parameter' to indicate what
			level has changed.

		Call setupListeners() when done if setupListeners is True
		"""
		currDevice = 'None'
		notFound = False
		levelIndex = -1 if level == 'Song' else self.lomParNames.index(level) \
												- 1
		validDevice = 'None'
		# track is treated like a chain for this purpose
		chainInfo = self.SongInfo['tracks']
		chainPar = getattr(self.ownerComp.par, 'Track', None)
		if chainPar is not None:
			chainPar.enable = True
		# Traverse track/device/chain etc.
		while chainPar is not None:
			if chainPar.enable or chainPar.name == 'Track':
				self._FinalChainPar.val = chainPar.name
				#self._FinalDevicePar.val = None
			chainIndex = self.lomParNames.index(chainPar.name)
			if levelIndex < chainIndex:
				currChain = self.updateLOMPar(chainPar, chainInfo)
			else:
				currChain = self.getMenuLabel(chainPar)
			# Device
			deviceIndex = chainIndex + 1
			devicePar = getattr(self.ownerComp.par,
								self.lomParNames[deviceIndex], None)
			if devicePar is not None:
				if levelIndex < deviceIndex:
					if chainPar.eval() == 'Not_Found' or notFound:
						deviceInfo = None
						devicePar.enable = False
						notFound = True
					elif currChain == 'None' or currChain is None:
						deviceInfo = None
						# if devicePar.mode in (ParMode.CONSTANT, ParMode.BIND):
						# 	devicePar.val = 'None'
						devicePar.enable = False
					else:
						devicePar.enable = True
						deviceInfo = chainInfo[currChain]['devices']
						validDevice = 'None'
					currDevice = self.updateLOMPar(devicePar, deviceInfo)
				else:
					if currChain == 'None':
						currDevice = 'None'
						# if devicePar.mode in (ParMode.CONSTANT, ParMode.BIND):
						# 	devicePar.val = 'None'
						deviceInfo = None
					else:
						currDevice = self.getMenuLabel(devicePar)
						deviceInfo = chainInfo[currChain]['devices']
				if devicePar.enable or devicePar.name == 'Device':
					self._FinalDevicePar.val = devicePar.name
					# self._FinalChainPar.val = None
				if devicePar.enable and currDevice != 'None':
					validDevice = currDevice
					validDevicePar = devicePar
					validDeviceInfo = deviceInfo
				# Chains
				chainIndex = deviceIndex + 1
				if self.lomParNames[chainIndex] == 'Parameter':# \
								#or deviceInfo and not deviceInfo['chainType']:
					# end of chains
					chainPar = None
				else:
					chainPar = getattr(self.ownerComp.par,
									   self.lomParNames[chainIndex], None)
				if chainPar:
					if devicePar.eval() == 'Not_Found' or notFound:
						chainInfo = None
						chainPar.enable = False
						notFound = True
					elif currDevice == 'None' or currDevice is None \
									or not deviceInfo[currDevice]['chainType']:
						chainInfo = None
						# if chainPar.mode in (ParMode.CONSTANT, ParMode.BIND):
						# 	chainPar.val = 'None'
						chainPar.enable = False
					else:
						chainInfo = deviceInfo[currDevice]['chains']
						chainPar.enable = True
			else:
				chainPar = None
		aParPar = getattr(self.ownerComp.par, 'Parameter', None)  # Parameter
		if aParPar is not None:
			if validDevice == 'None' or validDevicePar.eval() == 'Not_Found':
				aParInfo = None
				aParPar.enable = False
			else:
				aParPar.enable = True
				aParInfo = validDeviceInfo[validDevice]['aPars']
			if levelIndex < self.lomParNames.index('Parameter'):
				self.updateLOMPar(aParPar, aParInfo)
		# update top level binds
		for parName in LOMPARS:
			self.updateLOMParBinds(parName)
		if setupListeners and self.ownerComp.par.Connect.eval():
			self.setupListeners()
		self.checkLOMParErrors()

	def updateLOMPar(self, lomPar, lomInfo):
		"""
		Updates a Live Object Model parameter
		lomPar is the parameter
		lomInfo is the associated dictionary from self.SongInfo

		 menuLabel is LO.name,
		 menuName is LO pointer or
		 	'None' if None
		 	'Not_Found' if  not found

		 if lomInfo is None, set val to 'None' with no other menu options
		 if lomPar is disabled, just return None
		"""
		# no info dict
		if not lomPar.enable:
			return None
		if lomInfo is None:
			menuNames = ['None']
			menuLabels = ['None']
			currVal = 'None'
		else:
			# create menu from info dict
			infoList = list(lomInfo.values())
			menuLabels = ['None'] + [info['name'] for info in infoList]
			menuNames = ['None'] + [info['ptr'] for info in infoList]
			currVal = 'None'
			# try to set value
			try:
				currVal = self.getMenuLabel(lomPar)\
											if lomPar is not None else None
			except:
				if lomPar.mode in (ParMode.CONSTANT, ParMode.BIND):
					currVal = 'None'
			# chop off " (Not Found)" if this is a not found entry
			if lomPar.eval() == 'Not_Found' \
										and currVal.endswith(' (Not Found)'):
				currVal = currVal[:-12]
			try:
				if currVal not in menuLabels:
					if lomPar.eval() != 'Not_Found' and lomPar.eval() in \
							self.TDAbletonComp.SongInfoByPtr['tracks'].keys():
						currVal = self.TDAbletonComp.SongInfoByPtr['tracks']\
												[lomPar.eval()]['name']
					elif lomPar.mode in (ParMode.CONSTANT, ParMode.BIND):
						currVal += ' (Not Found)'
						menuLabels.insert(0, currVal)
						menuNames.insert(0, 'Not_Found')
			except:
				pass
		lomPar.menuNames = menuNames
		lomPar.menuLabels = menuLabels

		topMaster = TDF.bindChain(lomPar, parsOnly=True)[-1]
		topMaster.menuNames = menuNames
		topMaster.menuLabels = menuLabels
		if lomPar.mode in (ParMode.CONSTANT, ParMode.BIND):
			self.setMenuLabel(lomPar, currVal)
		if topMaster.mode in (ParMode.CONSTANT, ParMode.BIND):
			self.setMenuLabel(topMaster, currVal)
		return currVal

	def checkLOMParErrors(self, clear=True, disconnect=True, parNames=None):
		"""
		Check self.lomParNames for errors... If any are set to Not Found, raise
		Error. If any are not set to Not Found, but have a Not Found option,
		remove the Not Found option.

		clear: clear current errors first
		disconnect: disconnect if error
		parNames: list of parNames to check, if None check all standard LOM pars
		"""
		if clear:
			self.SetError('')
		if parNames is None:
			parNames = self.lomParNames
		if not self.TDAbletonComp.Connected or \
										not self.ownerComp.par.Connect.eval():
			return
		for lomParName in parNames:
			par = getattr(self.ownerComp.par, lomParName, None)
			if par is not None:
				if par.eval() == 'Not_Found':
					self.SetError(par.label + ": " + self.getMenuLabel(par))
					if disconnect:
						self.disconnect()
				elif 'Not_Found' in par.menuNames and \
							not par.eval() == 'Not_Found':
					val = par.eval()
					index = par.menuNames.index('Not_Found')
					menuLabels = par.menuLabels
					menuNames = par.menuNames
					menuLabels.pop(index)
					menuNames.pop(index)
					par.menuNames = menuNames
					par.menuLabels = menuLabels
					self.updateLOMParBinds(par)
					if par.mode in (ParMode.CONSTANT, ParMode.BIND):
						par.val = val

	def LOMParInfo(self, lomParName):
		"""
		Return infoDict for the LOM object selected in the given par.
		infoDict: { 'lomInfo': SongInfo entry for LOM object,
					'lomExpression': TDA Python expression to object
					'returnAddress': Standard address for OSC replies from TDA
					 }
		 	returns None if no such parameter
		 	returns 0 if parameter is disabled
		 	returns False if not found object is selected in menu
		 	returns {} if 'None' is selected in menu
		"""
		if lomParName is None or not hasattr(self.ownerComp.par, lomParName):
			return None
		lomPar = getattr(self.ownerComp.par, lomParName)
		if not lomPar.enable:
			return 0
		val = lomPar.eval()
		if val == 'Not_Found':
			return False
		elif val == 'None':
			return {}
		else:
			lomExpression = 'SONG'
			returnAddress = '/song'
			infoDict = self.SongInfo['tracks']
			# step through lomPars to create info
			if lomParName == 'Parameter':
				steps = self.lomParNames[:
							self.lomParNames.index(self.FinalDevicePar) + 1]
			else:
				steps = self.lomParNames[:self.lomParNames.index(lomParName)+1]
			for stepName in steps:
				stepPar = getattr(self.ownerComp.par, stepName)
				stepDict = infoDict[self.getMenuLabel(stepPar)]
				lomExpression += '.' + stepDict['expression']
				returnAddress += '/' + stepDict['name']
				if 'chains' in stepDict:
					infoDict = stepDict['chains']
				else:
					infoDict = stepDict['devices']
			if lomParName == 'Parameter':
				stepDict = stepDict['aPars'][self.getMenuLabel(lomPar)]
				lomExpression += '.' + stepDict['expression']
				returnAddress += '/' + stepDict['name']
			returnAddress = self.validChannelName(returnAddress)
			return {'lomInfo': stepDict,
					'lomExpression': lomExpression,
					'returnAddress': returnAddress}

	def mapPtrToPars(self, livePtr):
		livePtr = int(livePtr)
		if livePtr == -1:
			op.TDResources.op('popDialog').Open("Select a parameter in Live "
												"then try again",
												"No parameter selected",
												buttons=['OK'])
			return
		info = self.TDAbletonComp.ObjectInfoByPtr[int(livePtr)]['byPtr']
		hierarchy = []
		while info is not None and 'parentInfo' in info:
			hierarchy.insert(0, info)
			info = info['parentInfo']
		if len(hierarchy) > 1 and 'aPars' in hierarchy[-2] \
							and hierarchy[-1]['name'] in hierarchy[-2]['aPars']:
			parameter = hierarchy.pop(-1)
			if not hasattr(self.ownerComp.par, 'Parameter'):
				raise Exception(parent.ownerComp.path +
					" does not have the necessary Live "
					"Object Model parameters to map to the selected "
					"parameter.")
				return
		else:
			parameter = None
		for parName in self.lomParNames[:len(hierarchy)]:
			if not hasattr(self.ownerComp.par, parName):
				raise Exception(parent.ownerComp.path +
					" does not have the necessary Live "
					"Object Model parameters to map to the selected "
					"object.")
				return
		for parName in self.lomParNames:
			if hasattr(self.ownerComp.par, parName):
				self.setMenuLabel(getattr(self.ownerComp.par, parName), 'None')
		for i, parName in enumerate(self.lomParNames[:len(hierarchy)]):
			self.setMenuLabel(getattr(self.ownerComp.par, parName),
							  hierarchy[i]['name'])
			self.updateLOMPars(setupListeners=False)
		if parameter:
			self.setMenuLabel(self.ownerComp.par.Parameter, parameter['name'])

	# endregion

	# region Callbacks

	def onAbletonNotify(self, info):
		"""
		Callback from tdAbleton.
		info contains notificationType key and any other necessary info.
		"""
		notificationType = info['notificationType']
		if notificationType in ['songInfo', 'connected']:
			if info['notificationType'] == 'connected':
				self.OSCInCHOP.par.port = self.TDAbletonComp.par.Inport.eval()
				self.OSCInDAT.par.port = self.TDAbletonComp.par.Inport.eval()
				self.disconnect()
			self.SongInfo = self.TDAbletonComp.SongInfo
			for name in self.lomParNames:
				self.updateLOMParBinds(name, True)
			self.updateLOMPars()
		elif notificationType == 'disconnected':
			for name in self.lomParNames:
				self.updateLOMParBinds(name, False)
			self.disconnect()
		elif notificationType == 'lomNameChanged':
			for lomParName in self.lomParNames:
				lomPar = getattr(self.ownerComp.par, lomParName, None)
				if lomPar is not None:
					self.onLOMNameChanged(info, lomPar)
		elif notificationType == 'tdAbletonVersionChange':
			self.ownerComp.par.Version = info['version']
			self.ownerComp.par.Version.expr = ''
		elif notificationType == 'reinit':
			self.setTDAbletonComp()

	def onLOMNameChanged(self, info, lomPar):
		"""
		Do menu fixing for a LOM Par when LOM object name is changed.

		info: info about the changed LOM object. Must contain at least the ptr
				and the menu name to be used
		lomPar: par to check
		"""
		ptr = str(info['ptr'])
		if ptr in lomPar.menuNames:
			menuIndex = lomPar.menuNames.index(ptr)
			menuLabels = lomPar.menuLabels
			menuLabels[menuIndex] = info['name']
			lomPar.menuLabels = menuLabels
			self.updateLOMParBinds(lomPar)
			if lomPar.eval() == 'Not_Found' and \
							self.getMenuLabel(lomPar)[:-12] == info['name']:
				self.updateLOMPars()
			elif menuIndex == lomPar.menuIndex and \
					self.ownerComp.par.Connect.eval():
				self.setupListeners()

	def onReceiveOSCInfo(self, dat, rowIndex, message, msgBytes, timeStamp,
														address, args, peer):
		"""
		Got /info OSC message
		"""
		if address.endswith('/setterIndex'):
			# args: setterIndex, lomExpression, property, returnAddress, id,
			# 		extra
			key = tuple(args[1:6]) if args[5] else tuple(args[1:5])
			if key in self.Listeners:
				listener = self.Listeners[key]
				# if listener['setterIndex'] is not None:
				# 	debug('repeat set setterIndex', self.ownerComp.path)
				listener['setterIndex'] = args[0]
				if all([l['setterIndex'] is not None
											for l in self.Listeners.values()]):
					self.ListenersConfirmed = True
				# debug('setterIndex', listener)
				outPar = listener['outPar']
				channel = self.strippedChannel(key[2])
				if outPar is not None:
					# alter channel to adjust for stripsegments
					par = getattr(self.ownerComp.par, outPar.name)
					self.OutPars[outPar.name] = [
							par,
							channel,
							args[0], # setterIndex
							None # last ableton value or restoreAutoSync runObj
							]
					self.OutParNames.add(listener['outPar'].name)
					run("args[0].onOutputsChannelChange(args[1]," 
							"args[0].OSCInCHOP[args[1]]) "
						"if args[0].OSCInCHOP[args[1]] is not None else None",
						self, channel, delayFrames=1, delayRef=op.TDResources)
				else:
					run("args[0].onOutputsChannelInit(args[1]," 
							"args[0].OSCInCHOP[args[1]]) "
						"if args[0].OSCInCHOP[args[1]] is not None else None",
						self, channel, delayFrames=1, delayRef=op.TDResources)



	def onOutParChange(self, par):
		"""
		An 'outPar' value changed. Send it to the appropriate setter.
		"""
		outParInfo = self.OutPars[par.name]
		try:
			val = float(par.eval())
		except:
			val = float(par.menuIndex)
		if val != outParInfo[3] and outParInfo[2] != -1:
			self.sendOSC('/listener/set', [outParInfo[2], val])
			# turn off autoSync for one second
			if outParInfo[1] in self.AutoSyncChannels:
				del self.AutoSyncChannels[outParInfo[1]]
				outParInfo[3] = run("op(" + str(self.ownerComp.id) +
								").ext.TDAbletonCompBaseExt.restoreAutoSync('"
								+ par.name + "')",
								delayMilliSeconds=1000, delayRef=op.TDResources)
			else:
				if outParInfo[3] is not None:
					outParInfo[3].remainingMilliseconds = 1000

	def restoreAutoSync(self, outParName):
		if outParName in self.OutPars:
			outParInfo = self.OutPars[outParName]
			self.AutoSyncChannels[outParInfo[1]] = outParName
			if outParInfo[0].mode in (ParMode.CONSTANT, ParMode.BIND):
				outParInfo[0].val = self.OSCInCHOP[outParInfo[1]].eval()
			outParInfo[3] = outParInfo[0].eval()

	def setListener(self, listenerKey, val, ignoreErrors=False):
		listener = self.Listeners[listenerKey]
		try:
			if listener['setterIndex'] > 0:
				self.sendOSC('/listener/set', [listener['setterIndex'], val])
		except:
			if ignoreErrors:
				pass
			else:
				raise Exception('Setter index not found for listener: ' 
														+ str(listenerKey))

	def onOutputsChannelInit(self, channel, value):
		"""
		Only for channels without outPars.
		"""
		pass

	def onOutputsChannelChange(self, channel, value):
		"""
		An OSC output channel has changed.
		"""
		try:
			outParInfo = self.OutPars[self.AutoSyncChannels[channel]]
			if outParInfo[0].mode not in (ParMode.EXPRESSION, ParMode.EXPORT):
				outParInfo[3] = value
				outParInfo[0].val = value
		except:
			pass

	def clearOSCIn(self):
		self.OSCInCHOP.par.resetpulse.pulse()

	def onParValueChange(self, par, val, prev):
		if par.name in self.lomParNames:
			self.clearOSCIn()
			self.updateLOMPars(par.name)
			self.checkLOMParErrors()
			# if self.Connected:
			# 	self.clearOSCIn()
		elif par.name == 'Tdabletoncomp':
			self.setTDAbletonComp()
		elif par.name == 'Autosync':
			if par.eval():
				if hasattr(self.ownerComp.par, 'Sync'):
					self.ownerComp.par.Sync.enable = False
				self.Sync()
			else:
				if hasattr(self.ownerComp.par, 'Sync'):
					self.ownerComp.par.Sync.enable = True
		elif par.name in ['Stripchopprefixsegments', 'Timesliceoscchops'] \
				or (par.name == 'Sendonly' and not par.eval()):
			self.clearOSCIn()
			if self.ownerComp.par.Connect:
				self.setupListeners()
		elif par.name == 'Connect':
			if par.eval():
				self.tryConnection()
			else:
				self.disconnect()


	def onParPulse(self, par):
		if par.name == 'Update':
			self.Update()
		elif par.name == 'Sync':
			self.Sync()
		elif par.name == 'Helppage':
			ui.viewFile('https://docs.derivative.ca/'
						'index.php?title=TDAbleton_System_Components')
		elif par.name == 'Clearchop':
			self.clearOSCIn()
			if self.ownerComp.par.Connect:
				self.setupListeners()
		# elif par.name == 'Promotenavigationpars':
		# 	self.promoteNavigationPars()

	def updateLOMParBinds(self, par, enable=None):
		if isinstance(par, str):
			try:
				lomPar = getattr(self.ownerComp.par, par)
			except:
				return
		else:
			lomPar = par
		if enable is None:
			enable = lomPar.enable

		bindChain = TDF.bindChain(lomPar, parsOnly=True)

		# recursive function to get all references too
		done = set()
		def updateBindReferences(targetPar):
			if targetPar in done:
				return
			done.add(targetPar)
			try:
				targetPar.enable = enable
				targetPar.menuNames = lomPar.menuNames
				targetPar.menuLabels = lomPar.menuLabels
			except:
				# builtin parameter or some other nonsense
				pass
			for bindRef in targetPar.bindReferences:
				if isinstance(bindRef, Par):
					updateBindReferences(bindRef)

		updateBindReferences(bindChain[-1])

	# endregion

	# region Utilities
	@staticmethod
	def ConvertIntToRGB(i):
		return(((i & 0xFF0000) >> 16) / 255,
				((i & 0x00FF00) >> 8) / 255,
				(i & 0x0000FF) / 255)

	@staticmethod
	def ConvertRGBToInt(r, g, b):
		return int(b * 255) + \
			 (int(g * 255) << 8) + \
			 (int(r * 255) << 16)
	# endregion

	pass

*����������������� # me - this DAT
# 
# dat - the DAT that received a message
# rowIndex - the row number the message was placed into
# message - an ascii representation of the data
#           Unprintable characters and unicode characters will
#           not be preserved. Use the 'bytes' parameter to get
#           the raw bytes that were sent.
# bytes - a byte array of the message.
# timeStamp - the arrival time component the OSC message
# address - the address component of the OSC message
# args - a list of values contained within the OSC message
# peer - a Peer object describing the originating message
#   peer.close()    #close the connection
#   peer.owner  #the operator to whom the peer belongs
#   peer.address    #network address associated with the peer
#   peer.port       #network port associated with the peer
#


def onReceiveOSC(dat, rowIndex, message, bytes, timeStamp, address, args, peer):
	if address.startswith('/info'):
		ext.TDAbletonExt.onReceiveOSCInfo(dat, rowIndex, message, bytes, timeStamp, address, args, peer)
	elif address.startswith('/debug'):
		ext.TDAbletonExt.onReceiveOSCDebug(dat, rowIndex, message, bytes, timeStamp, address, args, peer)
	elif address.startswith('/shell'):
		ext.TDAbletonExt.onReceiveOSCShellReply(dat, rowIndex, message, bytes, timeStamp, address, args, peer)

*�����������������$f# AbletonMIDIExt

import socket
from copy import deepcopy
TDF = op.TDModules.mod.TDFunctions
TDAbletonCompBaseExt = \
					mod('abletonBase/TDAbletonCompBaseExt').TDAbletonCompBaseExt

class AbletonMIDIExt(TDAbletonCompBaseExt):
	"""
	AbletonMIDIExt works with TDA_MIDI Max Device to get MIDI notes
	"""
	def __init__(self, ownerComp):
		# The component to which this extension is attached
		self.OSCDeviceInCHOP = ownerComp.op('oscin_device')
		self.NoteDataCHOP = ownerComp.op('noteData')
		self.DefaultNotesCHOP = ownerComp.op('defaultNotes')
		self.NoteSplitterCHOP = ownerComp.op('noteSplitter')
		self.NoteSplitterCHOP.clear()
		self.LastNoteSplitterCHOP = ownerComp.op('lastNoteSplitter')
		self.LastNoteSplitterCHOP.clear()
		TDF.createProperty(self, 'deviceOutAddress', '', dependable=True)
		TDAbletonCompBaseExt.__init__(self, ownerComp)
		self.ownerComp.par.Defaultnoterange1.enable = \
							self.ownerComp.par.Showdefaultnotechannels.eval()
		run('op(' + str(self.ownerComp.id) + \
									').ext.AbletonMIDIExt.checkDeviceEnable()'\
					' if op(' + str(self.ownerComp.id) + ') else None',
					delayFrames=1, delayRef=op.TDResources)

	def SendMIDI(self, *args):
		"""
		Send a MIDI command to the connected TDA MIDI device.
		Arguments are a MIDI command string and 0-2 int data items:
			'note' <pitch value> <velocity>
			'pressure' <aftertouch pressure> <pitch value>
			'control' <control value> <controller number>
			'program' <program change value>
			'aftertouch' <aftertouch pressure>
			'pitchbend' <pitchbend amount -1.0 to 1.0>
			'channel' <MIDI channel>
			'flush' (no arguments, stops all MIDI notes)
		"""
		try:
			self.TDAbletonComp.SendMaxOSC(self.deviceOutAddress, args)
		except:
			if self.deviceOutAddress == '':
				raise Exception(self.ownerComp.path +
											": TDA MIDI device not connected.")
			raise

	def updateLOMPar(self, lomPar, lomInfo):
		"""
		Apply filters to base method.
		Track will be filtered for midi tracks.
		Device will be filtered for TDA_MIDI M4L devices only

		Base method:
		Updates a Live Object Model parameter (Track, Device, Parameter)
		lomPar is the parameter
		lomInfo is the associated dictionary from self.SongInfo

		 menuLabel is LO.name, menuName is LO pointer or
		 	'None' if None
		 	'Not_Found' if  not found
		 if lomInfo is None, set val to 'None' with no other menu options
		"""
		newLOMInfo = dict()
		# only include tracks with midi inputs
		if lomPar.name == 'Track' and lomInfo:
			for chainInfo in lomInfo.values():
				if chainInfo['hasMIDIInput']:
					newLOMInfo[chainInfo['name']] = chainInfo
		# only include TDA_MIDI devices
		elif lomInfo and (lomPar.name == 'Device'
						or lomPar.name.endswith('device')):
			pickDevice = None
			for deviceInfo in lomInfo.values():
				if 'ID_TDA_MIDI' in deviceInfo['aPars'] \
						or deviceInfo['chainType']:
					newLOMInfo[deviceInfo['name']] = deviceInfo
					if pickDevice is None and not deviceInfo['chainType']:
						pickDevice = deviceInfo
			# use first available device if current value not found
			if pickDevice:
				label = self.getMenuLabel(lomPar)
				if label.endswith(' (Not Found)'):
					label = label[:-12]
				# if newLOMInfo and (label not in newLOMInfo):
				# 	# hack the valid device name into menu
				# 	labels = lomPar.menuLabels
				# 	labels[lomPar.menuIndex] = pickDevice['name']
				# 	lomPar.menuLabels = labels
		# include all chains
		elif lomPar.name.startswith('Chain'):
			newLOMInfo = lomInfo
		return TDAbletonCompBaseExt.updateLOMPar(self, lomPar, newLOMInfo)

	def setupListeners(self):
		TDAbletonCompBaseExt.setupListeners(self)

		chainInfo = self.LOMParInfo(self.FinalChainPar)
		self.ownerComp.par.Adddevice.enable = chainInfo \
											and self.FinalChainPar == 'Track'

		# make sure we have a valid ableton parameter
		deviceInfo = self.maxDeviceInfo
		if not deviceInfo:
			self.ownerComp.par.Deviceon = False
			self.ownerComp.par.Deviceon.enable = False
			self.ownerComp.par.Devicename = ''
			self.ownerComp.par.Devicename.enable = False
			self.OSCDeviceInCHOP.par.oscaddressscope = ''
			self.deviceOutAddress = ''
			self.checkDeviceEnable()
			return

		maxTrackID = self.MaxTrackID()
		lomExpression = deviceInfo['lomExpression']
		returnAddressBase = deviceInfo['returnAddress']

		# midi channel name
		chanName = '/midi/' + maxTrackID + '/' + deviceInfo['lomInfo']['name']
		midiScope = chanName + '/*'
		self.OSCDeviceInCHOP.par.oscaddressscope = midiScope

		self.deviceOutAddress = chanName

		# make sure midi send device has correct address.
		self.sendOSC('/tda/command', ['setDeviceNetworking', lomExpression])

		# other info from MIDI device
		ownerId = self.ownerComp.id
		# DeviceOn
		self.ownerComp.par.Deviceon.enable = True
		deviceOnAddress = returnAddressBase + '/Device_On/value'
		listener = (lomExpression + '.parameters[0]', 'value',
													deviceOnAddress, ownerId)
		self.addListener(listener, self.ownerComp.par.Deviceon)
		self.checkDeviceEnable()
		# name
		self.ownerComp.par.Devicename.enable = True
		self.ownerComp.par.Devicename = deviceInfo['lomInfo']['name']
		nameAddress = '/info/listener' + returnAddressBase + '/name'
		listener = (lomExpression, 'name', nameAddress, ownerId)
		self.addListener(listener)

	def onAbletonNotify(self, info):
		TDAbletonCompBaseExt.onAbletonNotify(self, info)
		# Set midi ports when connected
		if info['notificationType'] == 'connected':
			self.checkDeviceEnable()
			self.OSCDeviceInCHOP.par.port = self.TDAbletonComp.par.Inport.eval()
		elif info['notificationType'] == 'disconnected':
			self.checkDeviceEnable()
		elif info['notificationType'] == 'lomNameChanged':
			changedObj = info
			if str(changedObj['ptr']) == self.ownerComp.par.Device.eval():
				self.ownerComp.par.Devicename = changedObj['name']
				validName = self.validChannelName(changedObj['name'])
				if info['name'] != validName:
					self.setDeviceName(validName)

	def clearOSCIn(self):
		self.NoteSplitterCHOP.clear()
		self.OSCDeviceInCHOP.par.resetpulse.pulse()
		TDAbletonCompBaseExt.clearOSCIn(self)
		self.NoteDataCHOP.par.value0 = -1
		self.NoteDataCHOP.par.value1 = -1

	def setDefaultNoteRange(self):
		self.setDefaultRange('Defaultnoterange', 'note_')

	def setDefaultCCRange(self):
		self.setDefaultRange('Defaultccrange', 'control_')

	def setDefaultRange(self, parName, suffix):
		minPar = self.ownerComp.par[parName + '1']
		maxPar = self.ownerComp.par[parName + '2']
		channels = self.OSCDeviceInCHOP.chans()
		min = 100000
		max = -1
		for c in channels:			
			if (id := c.name.split('/')[-1]).startswith(suffix):
				num = int(id.split('_')[1])
				if num < min:
					min = num
				if num > max:
					max = num
		if max != -1:
			minPar.val = min
			maxPar.val = max

	def onParPulse(self, par):
		if par.name == 'Adddevice':
			if not self.TDAbletonComp.Connected:
				return
			TDAbletonCompBaseExt.addDevice(self, 'TDA_MIDI.amxd', 'TdaMIDI')
		elif par.name == 'Settorecentlyplayed':
			self.setDefaultNoteRange()
		elif par.name == 'Settorecentlyused':
			self.setDefaultCCRange()
		else:
			TDAbletonCompBaseExt.onParPulse(self, par)

	def onParValueChange(self, par, val, prev):
		if par.name == 'Devicename':
			# make sure device name is a valid channel name or there'll be
			# problems receiving data
			deviceInfo = self.maxDeviceInfo
			if deviceInfo:
				val = self.validChannelName(val)
				if par.eval() != val or deviceInfo['lomInfo']['name'] != val:
					self.setDeviceName(val)
		elif par.name == 'Connect':
			self.checkDeviceEnable()
			TDAbletonCompBaseExt.onParValueChange(self, par, val, prev)
		elif par.name == 'Stripdeviceprefixsegments':
			self.OSCDeviceInCHOP.par.resetpulse.pulse()
			if self.ownerComp.par.Connect:
				self.setupListeners()
		elif par.name == 'Showdefaultnotechannels':
			self.ownerComp.par.Defaultnoterange1.enable = par.eval()
		elif par.name == 'Showdefaultccchannels':
			self.ownerComp.par.Defaultccrange1.enable = par.eval()
		else:
			TDAbletonCompBaseExt.onParValueChange(self, par, val, prev)

	def checkDeviceEnable(self):
		self.OSCDeviceInCHOP.cook(force=True)
		self.OutCHOP.cook(force=True)

	def setDeviceName(self, name):
		deviceInfo = self.LOMParInfo(self.FinalDevicePar)
		command = deviceInfo['lomExpression'] + '.name = "' + name + '"'
		self.runRemoteCode(command)

	def doMIDICallback(self, address, args):
		channel, info = address.split('/')[-2:]
		channel = int(channel[2:])
		info = info.split('_')
		try:
			typeNumber = info[1]
		except:
			typeNumber = None
		parent().DoCallback("onMIDIEvent",
							{'address': address, 'eventValue': args[0],
							 'channel': channel, 'eventType': info[0],
							 'eventTypeNumber': typeNumber})

	@property
	def deviceOutAddressStripped(self):
		strips = self.ownerComp.par.Stripdeviceprefixsegments.eval()
		address = self.deviceOutAddress[1:]
		for i in range(strips):
			slash = address.find('/')
			if slash == -1:
				address = ''
			else:
				address = address[slash + 1:]
		if address:
			return address + '/'
		else:
			return ''

	@property
	def maxDeviceInfo(self):
		"""
		device par info
		:return: par info or None if invalid device or no device chosen.
		"""
		deviceInfo = self.LOMParInfo(self.FinalDevicePar)
		if deviceInfo and 'ID_TDA_MIDI' in deviceInfo['lomInfo']['aPars']:
			return deviceInfo

*�����������������# me - this DAT
# 
# dat - the DAT that received a message
# rowIndex - the row number the message was placed into
# message - an ascii representation of the data
#           Unprintable characters and unicode characters will
#           not be preserved. Use the 'bytes' parameter to get
#           the raw bytes that were sent.
# bytes - a byte array of the message.
# timeStamp - the arrival time component the OSC message
# address - the address component of the OSC message
# args - a list of values contained within the OSC message
# peer - a Peer object describing the originating message
#   peer.close()    #close the connection
#   peer.owner  #the operator to whom the peer belongs
#   peer.address    #network address associated with the peer
#   peer.port       #network port associated with the peer
#

callbackPar = parent().par.Enablecallbacks
splitNotePar = parent().par.Splittouchingnotes
noteSplitterCHOP = op('noteSplitter')
lastNotePar = parent().par.Lastnotedata
noteDataCHOP = op('noteData')
velocityPar = noteDataCHOP.par.name1
lastNoteSplitterCHOP = op('lastNoteSplitter')

def onReceiveOSC(dat, rowIndex, message, bytes, timeStamp, address, args, peer):
	if callbackPar.eval():
		ext.TDAbletonCompBaseExt.doMIDICallback(address, args)
	if splitNotePar.eval():
		chan = address[1:]
		if args[0] == 0:
			if noteSplitterCHOP[chan] is None:
				noteSplitterCHOP.appendChan(chan)
				run('args[0](args[1], args[2])', destroyNoteSplitChan, 
						noteSplitterCHOP, chan, delayFrames=1, 
						delayRef=op.TDResources)
			if lastNotePar.eval():
				vchan = velocityPar.eval()
				if lastNoteSplitterCHOP[vchan] is None:
					lastNoteSplitterCHOP.appendChan(vchan)
					run('args[0](args[1], args[2])', destroyNoteSplitChan, 
								lastNoteSplitterCHOP, vchan, delayFrames=1, 
								delayRef=op.TDResources)

def destroyNoteSplitChan(chop, name):
	try:
		chop[name].destroy()
	except:
		pass

*�����������������@f# AbletonRackExt

from distutils.log import debug
import socket
from copy import deepcopy

TDF = op.TDModules.mod.TDFunctions
TDAbletonCompBaseExt = \
	mod('abletonBase/TDAbletonCompBaseExt').TDAbletonCompBaseExt


class AbletonRackExt(TDAbletonCompBaseExt):
	"""
	AbletonLevelExt works with TDA_Level Max Device to get audio level data
	"""

	def __init__(self, ownerComp):
		# The component to which this extension is attached
		self.OSCDeviceInCHOP = ownerComp.op('oscin_device')
		self.OSCControlsInDAT = ownerComp.op('oscin_controls')
		self.OSCMacrosSendCHOP = ownerComp.op('macroSends')
		self.ChanNameCHOP = ownerComp.op('chanNames')
		self.InOutIndexes = []
		self.deviceChannel = None
		self.rackName = None
		self.deviceName = None
		self.isRackSet = False
		# can be multiple devices
		TDF.createProperty(self, 'SendDevices', {}, dependable=True)
		TDF.createProperty(self, 'MacroOSCSendAddresses', [], dependable='deep')
		TDAbletonCompBaseExt.__init__(self, ownerComp)

		# list of device parameters to track, by Ableton device par index:
		# 	['TD par name', 'osc_channel_par_name'] or ['name'] or None to skip
		self.devicePars = [['Deviceon', 'Device_On']]
		# info about the rack types. {rack device name: pulse parameter}
		self.midiRackInfo = {'TDA_Instrument_Rack': ownerComp.par.Addir,
							 'TDA_MIDI_Effect_Rack': ownerComp.par.Addmer}
		self.audioRackInfo = {'TDA_Audio_Effect_Rack': ownerComp.par.Addaer}
		self.rackInfo = {}
		self.rackInfo.update(self.midiRackInfo)
		self.rackInfo.update(self.audioRackInfo)
		self.oscDevicePars = [ownerComp.par.Macrososconly,
							  ownerComp.par.Oscinputchop,
							  ownerComp.par.Reorder]
		self.addRackPars = [ownerComp.par.Addaer, ownerComp.par.Addir,
							ownerComp.par.Addmer]

		run('op(' + str(self.ownerComp.id) + \
				').ext.TDAbletonCompBaseExt.checkDeviceEnable()'\
				' if op(' + str(self.ownerComp.id) + ')	else None',
			delayFrames=1, delayRef=op.TDResources)

	def updateLOMPar(self, lomPar, lomInfo):
		"""
		Apply filters to base method.
		Device will be filtered for TDA_Level M4L devices only

		Base method:
		Updates a Live Object Model parameter (Track, Device, Parameter)
		lomPar is the parameter
		lomInfo is the associated dictionary from self.SongInfo

		 menuLabel is LO.name, menuName is LO pointer or
		 	'None' if None
		 	'Not_Found' if  not found
		 if lomInfo is None, set val to 'None' with no other menu options
		"""
		# only include rack devices (not drum pads)
		if lomInfo and (lomPar.name == 'Device'
						or lomPar.name.endswith('device')):
			newLOMInfo = dict()
			for deviceInfo in lomInfo.values():
				if deviceInfo['chainType'] == 'chains':
					newLOMInfo[deviceInfo['name']] = deviceInfo
			# use first available device if current value not found
			label = self.getMenuLabel(lomPar)
			if label.endswith(' (Not Found)'):
				label = label[:-12]
			# if newLOMInfo and (label not in newLOMInfo):
			# 	# hack the valid device name into menu
			# 	labels = lomPar.menuLabels
			# 	labels[lomPar.menuIndex] = list(newLOMInfo.keys())[0]
			# 	lomPar.menuLabels = labels
		else:
			newLOMInfo = lomInfo
		return TDAbletonCompBaseExt.updateLOMPar(self, lomPar, newLOMInfo)

	@property
	def deviceInfo(self):
		"""
		device par info
		:return: par info or None if invalid device or no device chosen.
		"""
		deviceInfo = self.LOMParInfo(self.FinalDevicePar)
		if deviceInfo and deviceInfo['lomInfo']['chainType'] == 'chains':
			return deviceInfo
		else:
			return None

	def setupListeners(self):
		self.isRackSet = False
		self.OSCControlsInDAT.par.active = False
		self.InOutIndexes = []
		self.MacroOSCSendAddresses = []
		self.deviceChannel = None
		TDAbletonCompBaseExt.setupListeners(self)
		oldMacroLabels = []
		# default a bunch of stuff to off, turn it on later if it's valid
		for macroNum in range(1, 9):
			par = getattr(self.ownerComp.par, 'Macro' + str(macroNum))
			oldMacroLabels.append(par.label)
			par.label = 'Macro' + str(macroNum)
			par.enable = False
			par = getattr(self.ownerComp.par, 'Macroosc' + str(macroNum))
			par.enable = False
		for par in self.rackInfo.values():
			par.enable = False
		for par in self.oscDevicePars:
			par.enable = False
		# turn on add rack buttons if we're on a track
		if self.FinalChainPar == 'Track' \
				and self.LOMParInfo(self.FinalChainPar):
			for par in self.audioRackInfo.values():
				par.enable = True
			if self.LOMParInfo(self.FinalChainPar)['lomInfo']['hasMIDIInput']:
				for par in self.midiRackInfo.values():
					par.enable = True

		# check if we have a valid device using deviceInfo property above
		deviceInfo = self.deviceInfo
		if deviceInfo is None:
			self.onDeviceInvalid()
			return
		if ' ' in deviceInfo['lomInfo']['name']:
			self.SetError(
					"TDAbleton Rack Devices can't have spaces in their names",
					label='TDA Rack name')
		else:
			self.SetError('', label='TDA Rack name')

		self.SendDevices.clear()
		self._SendDevices.modified()
		self.isRackSet = True

		# set device networking for all devices in Rack
		rackInfo = deviceInfo['lomInfo']
		maxTrackID = self.MaxTrackID()
		deviceLomExpression = deviceInfo['lomExpression']
		returnAddressBase = deviceInfo['returnAddress']
		sendDeviceInfo = None
		sendDeviceExpr = None
		oscAddressScopes = []

		if rackInfo['chains']:
			chainInfo = next(iter(rackInfo['chains'].values()))
			if chainInfo['hasMIDIOutput']:
				deviceName = 'TDA_Rack_MIDI_OSC'
			else:
				deviceName = 'TDA_Rack_OSC'
			for chain in rackInfo['chains'].values():
				if self.SendDevices:
					break
				for chainDeviceName, device in chain['devices'].items():
					if 'ID_' + deviceName in device['aPars']:
						sendDeviceInfo = device
						sendDeviceExpr = deviceLomExpression + '.' + \
										 chain['expression'] + '.' + \
										 device['expression']
						self.SendDevices[sendDeviceExpr] = sendDeviceInfo
						break

			for expression, device in self.SendDevices.items():
				# device channel name
				self.deviceChannel = \
					'/rack/' + maxTrackID + '/' + rackInfo['name']
				oscAddressScopes.append(self.deviceChannel + '/*')
				self.sendOSC('/tda/command', ['setDeviceNetworking',
											  expression])

		self.OSCDeviceInCHOP.par.oscaddressscope = ' '.join(oscAddressScopes)

		if sendDeviceInfo:
			# other info from device
			ownerId = self.ownerComp.id
			# Parameters (only DeviceOn for rack)
			for aParNum, info in enumerate(self.devicePars[:1]):
				if info is None:
					continue
				if len(info) == 1:
					info.append(info[0])
				par = getattr(self.ownerComp.par, info[0])
				par.enable = True
				returnAddress = returnAddressBase + '/' + info[1] + '/value'
				listener = (deviceLomExpression + '.parameters['
							+ str(aParNum) + ']', 'value', returnAddress,
							ownerId)
				self.addListener(listener, par)

			macroAPars = list(rackInfo['aPars'].keys())
			# we have to search for indexes because names can change
			macroPars = []
			for aParName in macroAPars:
				macroPar = rackInfo['aPars'][aParName]
				if 0 < macroPar['index'] < 9:
					macroPars.append(macroPar)
			# macro controls
			channelNames = [''] * 8
			for macroPar in macroPars:
				index = str(macroPar['index'])
				par = getattr(self.ownerComp.par, 'Macro' + index)
				par.label = macroPar['name']
				self.updateMacroBinds(par,
									  oldMacroLabels[macroPar['index'] - 1])
				channelNames[macroPar['index'] - 1] = macroPar['name']
				par.readOnly = False
				if not self.ownerComp.par.Macrososconly:
					par.enable = True
					# value listener
					lomExpression = deviceLomExpression + '.' \
									+ macroPar['expression']
					valueAddress = returnAddressBase + '/' + \
								   self.validChannelName(macroPar['name']) \
								   + '/value'
					listener = (lomExpression, 'value',
								valueAddress, ownerId)
					self.addListener(listener, par, macroPar['min'],
									 macroPar['max'])
				else:
					par.enable = False
			self.ChanNameCHOP.par.Channelnames = ' '.join(
				[self.ChanNameStripped(self.deviceChannel + '/' +
									   self.validChannelName(name),
									   self.ownerComp.par
									   .Stripdeviceprefixsegments)
				 for name in channelNames])
			for par in self.oscDevicePars:
				par.enable = True
			oscControlAddressScopes = []
			for i in range(1, 9):
				inOutIndex = []
				par = getattr(self.ownerComp.par, 'Macroosc' + str(i))
				par.enable = True
				aPars = [sendDeviceInfo['aPars']['oscIn' + str(i)],
						 sendDeviceInfo['aPars']['oscOut' + str(i)]]
				for aPar in aPars:
					lomExpression = sendDeviceExpr + '.' + \
									aPar['expression']
					valueAddress = returnAddressBase + '/' + aPar['name'] \
								   + '/value'
					listener = (lomExpression, 'value', valueAddress,
								ownerId)
					oscControlAddressScopes.append(valueAddress)
					self.addListener(listener, useBaseOSC=False)
					inOutIndex.append(aPar['index'])
				self.InOutIndexes.append(inOutIndex)
				# Have to delay to prevent feedback loop on startup
				run('op(' + str(self.ownerComp.id) + ').ext.'
					'TDAbletonCompBaseExt.sendMacroOSCInOut(' + str(i) +
					')', delayMilliSeconds=1000, delayRef=op.TDResources)
				# self.sendMacroOSCInOut(i)
				sending = self.MacroOSCInOut(i)[0]
				if sending:
					self.MacroOSCSendAddresses.append(
						self.deviceChannel + '/macro' + str(i))
			self.OSCControlsInDAT.par.addscope = \
				' '.join(oscControlAddressScopes)
			self.OSCControlsInDAT.par.active = \
				self.ownerComp.par.Connect.eval()

		# name
		self.ownerComp.par.Devicename.enable = True
		self.ownerComp.par.Devicename = self.getMenuLabel(
			getattr(self.ownerComp.par, self.FinalDevicePar))

		# oscin ready to go
		self.checkDeviceEnable()
		self.onSendCountChanged()

	def onOutParChange(self, par):
		if par.name.startswith('Macro') and par.readOnly:
			return
		TDAbletonCompBaseExt.onOutParChange(self, par)

	def onDeviceInvalid(self):
		"""
		Call this when we don't have a valid device, to disable stuff
		:return:
		"""
		for info in self.devicePars:
			if info is not None:
				getattr(self.ownerComp.par, info[0]).enable = False
		self.ownerComp.par.Devicename = ''
		self.ownerComp.par.Devicename.enable = False
		self.OSCDeviceInCHOP.par.oscaddressscope = ''
		self.checkDeviceEnable()
		self.OSCControlsInDAT.par.active = False
		self.deviceOutAddress = ''

	def onAbletonNotify(self, info):
		TDAbletonCompBaseExt.onAbletonNotify(self, info)
		# Set device ports when connected
		if info['notificationType'] == 'connected':
			self.checkDeviceEnable()
			self.OSCDeviceInCHOP.par.port = self.TDAbletonComp.par.Inport.eval()
			self.OSCControlsInDAT.par.port = \
				self.TDAbletonComp.par.Inport.eval()
		elif info['notificationType'] == 'disconnected':
			self.checkDeviceEnable()
			self.onSendCountChanged()
		elif info['notificationType'] == 'lomNameChanged':
			deviceInfo = self.deviceInfo
			if not deviceInfo:
				return
			changedObj = info
			devicePtr = deviceInfo['lomInfo']['ptr']
			if changedObj['ptr'] == devicePtr:
				self.ownerComp.par.Devicename = changedObj['name']
				validName = self.validChannelName(changedObj['name'])
				if info['name'] != validName:
					self.setObjectName(validName)
			else:
				# check for rack macro name changes
				try:
					changedObj['parentInfo']['ptr']
				except:
					return
				if changedObj['parentInfo']['ptr'] == devicePtr:
					self.setupListeners()

	def clearOSCIn(self):
		self.OSCDeviceInCHOP.par.resetpulse.pulse()
		TDAbletonCompBaseExt.clearOSCIn(self)

	def onParPulse(self, par):
		if par.name in [p.name for p in self.rackInfo.values()]:
			if not self.TDAbletonComp.Connected:
				return
			for name, p in self.rackInfo.items():
				if p.name == par.name:
					break
			TDAbletonCompBaseExt.addDevice(self, name + '.adg', name)
		else:
			TDAbletonCompBaseExt.onParPulse(self, par)

	def onParValueChange(self, par, val, prev):
		if par.name == 'Devicename':
			# make sure device name is a valid channel name or there'll be
			# problems receiving data
			deviceInfo = self.deviceInfo
			if deviceInfo:
				val = self.validChannelName(val)
				if par.eval() != val or deviceInfo['lomInfo']['name'] != val:
					self.setObjectName(val)
		elif par.name == 'Output':
			self.updateLOMPars()
		elif par.name == 'Connect':
			self.checkDeviceEnable()
			TDAbletonCompBaseExt.onParValueChange(self, par, val, prev)
		elif par.name == 'Stripdeviceprefixsegments':
			self.OSCDeviceInCHOP.par.resetpulse.pulse()
			if self.ownerComp.par.Connect:
				self.setupListeners()
		elif par.name == 'Macrososconly':
			self.setupListeners()
		elif par.name.startswith('Macroosc'):
			self.sendMacroOSCInOut(int(par.name[-1]))
			self.setupListeners()
		else:
			TDAbletonCompBaseExt.onParValueChange(self, par, val, prev)

	def checkDeviceEnable(self):
		self.OutCHOP.cook(force=True)
		self.OSCDeviceInCHOP.cook(force=True)

	def setObjectName(self, name, lomExpression=None):
		"""
		Change device's name
		:param name: new name
		:param lomExpression: expression to object or None to use FinalDevicePar
		:return:
		"""
		if not self.ownerComp.par.Connect:
			return
		if lomExpression is None:
			lomExpression = self.LOMParInfo(
				self.FinalDevicePar)['lomExpression']
		command = lomExpression + '.name = "' + name + '"'
		self.runRemoteCode(command)

	menuIndex2Tuple = {0: [1, 0], 1: [0, 1], 2: [1, 1], 3: [0, 0]}

	def MacroOSCInOut(self, macroNum):
		"""
		Get a list with [in, out] bools for in and out states of OSC device
		:param macroNum: 1-8
		:return: [in, out] bools
		"""
		par = getattr(self.ownerComp.par, 'Macroosc' + str(macroNum))
		return self.menuIndex2Tuple[par.menuIndex]

	def SetMacroOSCInOut(self, macroNum, inOut, value):
		"""
		Set the menu entry properly based on one of the elements
		:param macroNum: 1-9
		:param inOut: 0 for in, 1 for out
		:param value: boolean
		"""
		currentValue = self.MacroOSCInOut(macroNum)
		newValue = currentValue.copy()
		newValue[inOut] = value
		if newValue != currentValue:
			par = getattr(self.ownerComp.par, 'Macroosc' + str(macroNum))
			for index, pair in self.menuIndex2Tuple.items():
				if newValue == pair:
					par.menuIndex = index
					return

	def onSendCountChanged(self):
		if self.deviceChannel:
			offMacros = ['1', '2', '3', '4', '5', '6', '7', '8']
			if self.ownerComp.par.Connect and self.TDAbletonComp.Connected:
				for chan in self.OSCMacrosSendCHOP.chans():
					macroNum = chan.name[-1]
					macroAddress = self.deviceChannel + '/control' + macroNum
					self.TDAbletonComp.SendMaxOSC(macroAddress, [1])
					debug(chan.name)
					if macroNum in offMacros:
						offMacros.remove(macroNum)
					macroPar = getattr(self.ownerComp.par, 'Macro' + macroNum)
					macroPar.readOnly = True
			for macroNum in offMacros:
				macroAddress = self.deviceChannel + '/control' + macroNum
				self.TDAbletonComp.SendMaxOSC(macroAddress, [0])
				macroPar = getattr(self.ownerComp.par, 'Macro' + macroNum)
				macroPar.readOnly = False

	def sendMacroOSCInOut(self, macroNum):
		"""
		Send the values from Component Macro OSC parameter to Live
		:param macroNum: 1-8
		"""
		if not self.ownerComp.par.Connect:
			return

		settings = self.MacroOSCInOut(macroNum)
		sendDeviceExpr, sendDevice = next(iter(self.SendDevices.items()))
		for inOut in range(2):
			command = sendDeviceExpr + '.parameters[' \
					  + str(self.InOutIndexes[macroNum - 1][inOut]) \
					  + '].value = ' + str(settings[inOut])
			self.runRemoteCode(command)

	def onControlsChange(self, address, args):
		macroNum = int(address[-7])
		inOut = 0 if address[-8] == 'n' else 1
		self.SetMacroOSCInOut(macroNum, inOut, args[0])

	@property
	def deviceOutAddressStripped(self):
		strips = self.ownerComp.par.Stripdeviceprefixsegments.eval()
		address = self.deviceOutAddress[1:]
		for i in range(strips):
			slash = address.find('/')
			if slash == -1:
				address = ''
			else:
				address = address[slash + 1:]
		if address:
			return address + '/'
		else:
			return ''

	def updateMacroBinds(self, par, oldLabel):
		if oldLabel == par.label:
			return
		bindChain = TDF.bindChain(par, parsOnly=True)
		done = set()
		def updateBindReferences(targetPar):
			if targetPar in done:
				return
			done.add(targetPar)
			if targetPar.label == oldLabel:
				try:
					targetPar.label = par.label
				except:
					# builtin parameter or some other nonsense
					pass
			for bindRef in targetPar.bindReferences:
				if isinstance(bindRef, Par):
					updateBindReferences(bindRef)

		updateBindReferences(bindChain[-1])
